IS 383 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF IS 383 : 1970 COARSE AND FINE AGGREGATES FROM NATURAL SOURCES FOR CONCRETE (Second Revision)
IS 383 : 1970.scope: This standard specifies requirements for aggregates, including coarse and fine aggregates, derived from natural sources for use in the production of structural concrete and mass concrete works. It covers criteria for durability, soundness, and grading of aggregates for different concrete applications.

IS 2116 : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2116 : 1980 SAND FOR MASONRY MORTARS (First Revision)
IS 2116 : 1980.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for naturally occurring sands, crushed stone sands, and crushed gravel sands used in masonry mortars. It outlines quality criteria including freedom from deleterious materials and particle size grading limits. The standard ensures sand durability and proper grading for optimal mortar performance.

IS 9142 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9142 : 1979 ARTIFICIAL LIGHTWEIGHT AGGREGATES FOR CONCRETE MASONRY UNITS
IS 9142 : 1979.scope: This standard specifies requirements for artificial lightweight aggregates used in concrete masonry units, with emphasis on lightness in mass. It covers grading, bulk density, uniformity, and deleterious substances for aggregates like foamed blast furnace slag, clay, and cinder. The aggregates are intended for use in concrete masonry units.

IS 269 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 269 : 1989 ORDINARY PORTLAND CEMENT, 33 GRADE (Fourth Revision)
IS 269 : 1989.scope: Covers the manufacture and chemical and physical requirements of 33 grade ordinary Portland cement. It specifies chemical composition, fineness, soundness, setting time, compressive strength, and delivery specifications for this grade of cement.

IS 455 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 455 : 1989 PORTLAND SLAG CEMENT (Fourth Revision)
IS 455 : 1989.scope: Covers the manufacture and chemical and physical requirements for Portland slag cement. It specifies limits for chemical components like magnesium oxide, sulphur trioxide, and chloride content, as well as physical properties such as fineness, soundness, setting time, and compressive strength. The standard also addresses delivery and packaging specifications for the cement product.

IS 1489 (PART1) : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1489 (PART1) : 1991 PORTLAND POZZOLANA CEMENT PART 1 FLY ASH BASED
IS 1489 (PART1) : 1991.scope: Covers the manufacture, physical and chemical requirements of Portland pozzolana cement using only fly ash pozzolana.

IS 1489 (PART 2) : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF PORTLAND POZZOLANA CEMENT PART 2 CALCINED CLAY BASED
IS 1489 (PART 2) : 1991.scope: This standard covers the manufacture, physical and chemical requirements of Portland pozzolana cement manufactured by using calcined clay or a mixture of calcined clay and fly ash pozzolana. It specifies the raw materials, chemical and physical requirements, and delivery specifications for this type of cement.

IS 3466 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF MASONRY CEMENT (Second Revision)
IS 3466 : 1988.scope: This standard specifies requirements for masonry cement to be used for all general purposes where mortars for masonry are required, including structural concrete, flooring, and foundation work or reinforced and prestressed concrete works.

IS 6452 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6452 : 1989 HIGH ALUMINA CEMENT FOR STRUCTURAL USE (First Revision)
IS 6452 : 1989.scope: This standard covers the manufacture and specific requirements for high alumina cement (HAC) as a structural building material in colder regions of the country where ambient temperature is continuously 18°C and below. It notes that HAC is mainly a refractory cement but may be used as a structural material in cold regions due to high early strength development, with restrictions on its use.

IS 6909 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF SUPERSULPHATED CEMENT (First Revision)
IS 6909 : 1990.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the composition, manufacture, and testing of supersulphated cement (SSC). It covers applications in aggressive environments such as marine works, mass concrete jobs, and chemical works with sulphates. The cement is suitable for use in conditions with high sulphate exposure, provided temperature is below 40°C.

IS 8041 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8041 : 1990 RAPID HARDENING PORTLAND CEMENT (Second Revision)
IS 8041 : 1990.scope: This standard specifies the manufacture, chemical, and physical requirements for rapid hardening Portland cement. It clarifies that 'rapid hardening' should not be confused with 'quick setting' and references related standards for detailed information. The cement must meet specific fineness, soundness, setting time, and compressive strength requirements.

IS 8042 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF WHITE PORTLAND CEMENT (Second Revision)
IS 8042 : 1989.scope: White Portland cement is generally used for architectural and decorative purposes and is made from raw materials containing very little iron oxide and magnesium oxide. It is intended for non-structural use.

IS 8043 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF HYDROPHOBIC PORTLAND CEMENT (Second Revision)
IS 8043 : 1991.scope: This standard covers the manufacture, chemical, and physical requirements of hydrophobic Portland cement, which is obtained by intergrinding 33 grade ordinary Portland cement with hydrophobic agents to impart water-repelling properties. It is designed for use in construction applications where water resistance is critical, and should not be confused with waterproofing cements.

IS 8112 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8112 : 1989 43 GRADE ORDINARY PORTLAND CEMENT (First Revision)
IS 8112 : 1989.scope: This specification covers the requirements of ordinary Portland Cement for uses such as manufacture of prestressed concrete railway sleepers and precast products.

IS 12269 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12269 : 1987 53 GRADE ORDINARY PORTLAND CEMENT
IS 12269 : 1987.scope: This standard specifies the manufacture, chemical and physical requirements for 53 grade ordinary Portland cement. It is intended for use in specialized concrete works such as prestressed concrete and certain precast concrete items requiring higher compressive strength than ordinary Portland cement.

IS 12330 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12330 : 1988 SULPHATE RESISTING PORTLAND CEMENT
IS 12330 : 1988.scope: This standard covers the manufacture, chemical and physical requirements, and testing of sulphate resisting Portland cement, which is designed to be used in structural concrete where supersulfated cement is not recommended, especially when temperatures are below 40°C. It is produced by restricting the amount of tricalcium aluminate and blending materials like granulated blast furnace slag and calcium sulphate.

IS 2185 (PART 1) : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2185 (PART 1) : 1979 CONCRETE MASONRY UNITS PART 1 HOLLOW AND SOLID CONCRETE BLOCKS (Second Revision)
IS 2185 (PART 1) : 1979.scope: This standard covers requirements for hollow and solid concrete masonry units used in construction of load-bearing and non-load-bearing walls, partitions, panel walls, and as backing for other types of facing materials, garden walls, chimneys, and as fillers in concrete joist floor construction and shuttering for beams, columns and lintels.

IS 2185 (PART 2) : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2185 (PART 2) : 1983 CONCRETE MASONRY UNITS PART 2 HOLLOW AND SOLID LIGHTWEIGHT CONCRETE BLOCKS
IS 2185 (PART 2) : 1983.scope: This standard covers lightweight concrete masonry units used in load-bearing and non-load-bearing walls, including hollow and solid blocks with specific dimensions and tolerances. It classifies blocks into grades A and B based on their use in damp-proof courses and exterior walls. The standard also specifies physical requirements such as compressive strength, water absorption, and density for different types and grades of blocks.

IS 2185 (PART 3) : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2185 (PART 3) : 1984 CONCRETE MASONRY UNITS PART 3 AUTOCLAVED CELLULAR (AERATED) CONCRETE BLOCKS
IS 2185 (PART 3) : 1984.scope: Covers the requirements of autoclaved cellular (aerated) concrete blocks having density up to 1000 kg/m³. These blocks are manufactured using autoclaved cement products, sandlime bricks, or other similar materials cured at high temperatures. The standard specifies dimensions, tolerances, physical properties, and classification into two grades for use in masonry applications.

IS 4996 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF REINFORCED CONCRETE FENCE POSTS (First Revision)
IS 4996 : 1984.scope: This standard covers requirements for reinforced concrete fence posts, including classifications such as line posts, strainer posts, and struts or braces. It provides guidelines for shape, dimensions, tolerances, and strength tests for these posts used in post-and-wire fencing systems. The standard also includes recommendations for manufacturing and erection, with additional details in appendices.

IS 5751 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5751 : 1984 PRECAST CONCRETE COPING BLOCKS (First Revision)
IS 5751 : 1984.scope: This standard covers the requirements for precast concrete coping blocks, including materials, workmanship, and essential dimensions. These blocks serve as a defense against moisture entry into hollow concrete block walls and must resist lateral displacement and thermal/moisture movements while being durable.

IS 5758 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF PRECAST CONCRETE KERBS (First Revision)
IS 5758 : 1984.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for precast concrete units including kerbs, channels, edgings, quadrants, and gutter aprons for use in carriageways and footways. It details dimensions, tolerances, and testing procedures for these units. The standard covers standard sections and typical sections of concrete kerbs, channels, edgings, quadrants, and gutters.

IS 5820 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5820 : 1970 PRECAST CONCRETE CABLE COVERS
IS 5820 : 1970.scope: This standard specifies requirements for reinforced and unreinforced precast concrete cable covers used in underground power cables. It covers classification, dimensions, tolerances, and test strength criteria for different voltage applications. The covers are designed to protect cables from mechanical damage and environmental factors.

IS 6072 : 1971.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6072 : 1971 AUTOCLAVED REINFORCED CELLULAR CONCRETE WALL SLABS
IS 6072 : 1971.scope: This standard covers autoclaved reinforced cellular concrete wall slabs with density above 450 kg/m³ and up to 1000 kg/m³. These slabs are made from an inorganic binder, silicic acid, gas-generating material, and steam-cured under high pressure. They are used as structural wall elements in buildings.

IS 6073 : 1971.title: SUMMARY OF AUTOCLAVED REINFORCED CELLULAR CONCRETE FLOOR AND ROOF SLABS
IS 6073 : 1971.scope: This standard covers autoclaved reinforced cellular concrete floor and roof slabs with density above 450 and up to 1000 kg/m³. It specifies requirements for materials, classification, dimensions, physical properties, and finish. The slabs are designed for structural applications in floors and roofs.

IS 6523 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6523 : 1983 PRECAST REINFORCED CONCRETE DOOR AND WINDOW FRAMES (First Revision)
IS 6523 : 1983.scope: This standard covers the requirements for precast reinforced concrete door and window frames, specifying maximum opening width of 2.25 m and cross-sectional dimensions for single and double shutter doors. It outlines material specifications including cement, aggregates, and concrete strength, and references related standards for manufacturing, fixing, and erection.

IS 9893 : 1981.title: SUMMARY OF PRECAST CONCRETE BLOCKS FOR LINTELS AND SILLS
IS 9893 : 1981.scope: This standard covers the requirements for precast concrete lintels and sills, including their shape, dimensions, materials, and installation methods for use in masonry walls for door, window, and ventilator openings.

IS 10388 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 10388 : 1982 CORRUGATED COIR, WOODWOOL, CEMENT ROOFING SHEETS
IS 10388 : 1982.scope: This standard covers requirements for corrugated roofing sheets made from coir, woodwool, and cement. It specifies materials, dimensions, and physical properties for these sheets, promoting the use of indigenous building materials. The sheets may be plain or corrugated and are manufactured by mixing coir, woodwool, and cement in suitable proportions.

IS 12440 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12440 : 1988 PRECAST CONCRETE STONE MASONRY BLOCKS
IS 12440 : 1988.scope: This standard specifies requirements for precast concrete stone masonry blocks used in the construction of load-bearing and non-load-bearing walls. It covers dimensions, tolerances, physical properties, and classification of these blocks, including special earthquake-resistant blocks with semi-circular recesses.

IS 12592 : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF PRECAST CONCRETE MANHOLE COVER AND FRAME
IS 12592 : 2002.scope: This standard specifies requirements for precast steel reinforced cement concrete manhole covers and frames intended for use in sewerage and water drainage systems. It defines grades, shapes, dimensions, physical requirements, and load test specifications for these units.

IS 13990 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13990 : 1994 PRECAST REINFORCED CONCRETE PLANKS AND JOISTS FOR ROOFING AND FLOORING
IS 13990 : 1994.scope: This standard covers the requirements for precast reinforced concrete planks and joists used in the construction of roofs and floors. It specifies dimensions, tolerances, design criteria, and reinforcement details for these components. The standard applies to planks up to 1.5 m long and joists for residential buildings.

IS 14143 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14143 : 1994 PREFABRICATED BRICK PANEL AND PARTIALLY PRECAST CONCRETE JOIST FOR FLOORING AND ROOFING
IS 14143 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements for prefabricated brick panels and partially precast concrete joists used in flooring and roofing applications. It covers dimensions, reinforcement, tolerances, and testing procedures for these structural components. The standard is intended for use in residential and commercial building construction.

IS 14201 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14201 : 1994 PRECAST REINFORCED CONCRETE CHANNEL UNITS FOR CONSTRUCTION OF FLOORS AND ROOFS
IS 14201 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements for precast reinforced concrete channel units used in the construction of floors and roofs. The units are designed to be interlocked for easy assembly and are typically up to 4.5 m in length. They are manufactured with specific dimensions and reinforcement to ensure structural integrity and ease of installation.

IS 14241 : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14241 : 1995 PRECAST REINFORCED CONCRETE L-PANEL FOR ROOFING
IS 14241 : 1995.scope: This standard covers the requirements for prefabricated reinforced concrete L-panels used for making roofs for buildings. It also includes requirements for prefabricated reinforced concrete channel units to be used along with L-panels in roof construction.

IS 459 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 459 : 1992 CORRUGATED AND SEMI-CORRUGATED ASBESTOS CEMENT SHEETS (Third Revision)
IS 459 : 1992.scope: Covers corrugated and semi-corrugated asbestos cement sheets designed to provide structural weather-exposed surfaces of roofs and building walls of industrial, residential, agricultural, commercial, and institutional buildings, and for decorative purposes.

IS 1592 : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF ASBESTOS CEMENT PRESSURE PIPES (Fourth Revision)
IS 1592 : 2003.scope: This standard covers requirements for asbestos cement pressure pipes and joints intended for use under pressure. It defines conditions of manufacture, classification, characteristics, and acceptance tests applicable to these products. Pipes for sewerage and drainage are covered by IS 6908, while building pipes and fittings are covered by IS 1626.

IS 1626 (PART 1) : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF ASBESTOS CEMENT BUILDING PIPES AND PIPE FITTINGS, GUTTERS, AND ROOF FITTINGS PART 1 PIPES AND PIPE FITTINGS (Second Revision)
IS 1626 (PART 1) : 1994.scope: This standard covers requirements for socketed asbestos cement pipes and fittings, including single socketed pipes, bends, junctions, and various types of heads and connectors, intended for use in rainwater, soil, waste, and ventilating pipes in building construction.

IS 1626 (PART 2) : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1626 (PART 2) : 1994 ASBESTOS CEMENT BUILDING PIPES AND PIPE FITTINGS, GUTTERS AND ROOF FITTINGS PART 2 GUTTERS AND GUTTER FITTINGS (Second Revision)
IS 1626 (PART 2) : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements for asbestos cement gutters and their fittings used in buildings, including dimensional and physical specifications, workmanship, and tolerances for various types such as valley, boundary wall, and half round gutters.

IS 1626 : 1984.title: ASBESTOS CEMENT BUILDING PIPES AND PIPE FITTINGS, GUTTERS AND GUTTER FITTINGS PART 3 ROOF FITTINGS
IS 1626 : 1984.scope: This standard covers requirements for asbestos cement roofing fittings used with corrugated and semi-corrugated asbestos cement sheets. It specifies shapes, dimensions, tolerances, and physical requirements for various roof fittings such as ridges, eaves, barge boards, and expansion joints. The fittings must be free from visual defects and impermeable when tested.

IS 2096 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF ASBESTOS CEMENT FLAT SHEETS (First Revision)
IS 2096 : 1992.scope: This standard covers requirements for asbestos cement flat sheets, including composition, dimensions, tolerances, and tests. It differentiates these sheets from autoclaved silica asbestos cement sheets covered under IS 13000:1990. The standard specifies classification, thickness, length, width, and mechanical properties such as bending strength and density.

IS 2098 : 1997.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2098 : 1997 ASBESTOS CEMENT BUILDING BOARDS (First Revision)
IS 2098 : 1997.scope: This standard covers requirements for composition, dimensions, and testing of asbestos cement building boards. It specifies tolerances and classification for different classes of boards. The standard does not cover asbestos cement flat sheets and silica asbestos cement flat sheets, which are subject to different specifications.

IS 6908 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6908 : 1991 ASBESTOS CEMENT PIPES AND FITTINGS FOR SEWERAGE AND DRAINAGE (First revision)
IS 6908 : 1991.scope: This standard specifies requirements for asbestos cement pipes and fittings suitable for gravity flow at atmospheric pressure, intended for sewerage and drainage applications. The pipes and fittings shall be seamless, compact, and homogeneous with a smooth internal surface and flush joints.

IS 8870 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8870 : 1978 ASBESTOS CEMENT CABLE CONDUITS AND TROUGHS
IS 8870 : 1978.scope: Covers asbestos cement cable conduits and asbestos cement cable troughs of 50 to 150 mm diameter together with plastic couplings and asbestos cement collars with rubber rings. These are intended to accommodate paper insulated telecommunication and power cables. Also covers asbestos cement cable troughs of 100×100 mm to 300×300 mm size together with bends and union clips for use at ground level and above ground level for carrying cables.

IS 9627 : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9627 : 1980 ASBESTOS CEMENT PRESSURE PIPES (LIGHT DUTY)
IS 9627 : 1980.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the manufacture, classification, dimensions, tests, and acceptance criteria for asbestos cement pressure pipes of light duty, specifically for classes 5 and 10. It covers physical properties such as bursting and crushing strength, hydraulic test pressures, and joint types.

IS 13000 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF SILICA ASBESTOS - CEMENT FLAT SHEETS
IS 13000 : 1990.scope: This standard specifies requirements for materials, classification, dimensions, and tests for silica asbestos-cement flat sheets. It covers bending stress, density, and appearance criteria for classification. The sheets are intended for construction applications requiring flat, uniform, and durable panels.

IS 13008 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13008 : 1990 SHALLOW CORRUGATED ASBESTOS CEMENT SHEETS
IS 13008 : 1990.scope: Covers the requirements for materials, dimensions and tests for shallow corrugated asbestos cement sheets.

IS 458 : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF IS 458 : 2003 PRECAST CONCRETE PIPES (WITH AND WITHOUT REINFORCEMENT) – SPECIFICATION (Fourth Revision)
IS 458 : 2003.scope: This standard specifies requirements for reinforced and unreinforced precast concrete pipes used in water mains, sewers, culverts, and irrigation. It also covers collar requirements for these pipes and classifies them as per clause 4.1 of the standard.

IS 784 : 2001.title: SUMMARY OF IS 784 : 2001 PRESTRESSED CONCRETE PIPES (INCLUDING SPECIALS) (Second Revision)
IS 784 : 2001.scope: This standard covers prestressed concrete pipes, including specials, with nominal internal diameters ranging from 200 mm to 2500 mm. These pipes are manufactured with a concrete core, longitudinally prestressed with high tensile steel wire, and coated with cement mortar to withstand internal pressure and external design loads.

IS 4350 : 1967.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4350 : 1967 CONCRETE POROUS PIPES FOR UNDER DRAINAGE
IS 4350 : 1967.scope: This standard specifies requirements for concrete porous pipes used in drainage systems. The pipes have nominal internal diameters ranging from 80 to 900 mm and are manufactured with three types of joints: butt, rebated, or ogee. The standard covers dimensions, tolerances, and testing procedures for these pipes.

IS 7319 : 1974.title: SUMMARY OF PERFORATED CONCRETE PIPES
IS 7319 : 1974.scope: This standard covers requirements for perforated non-reinforced concrete pipes used in underdrainage work, specifically for infiltration galleries and reclaiming waterlogged areas. These pipes may be reinforced by mutual agreement between purchaser and supplier.

IS 7322 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 7322 : 1985 SPECIALS FOR STEEL CYLINDER REINFORCED CONCRETE PIPES
IS 7322 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements and test methods for steel cylinder reinforced concrete pipe specials conforming to IS 1916:1989, with nominal internal diameters from 200 to 1800 mm. It covers spigot and socket ends, plain or slip ends for field welding, and flanged ends for valve connections. These specials are designed for use in construction applications requiring durable, reinforced concrete piping systems.

IS 1834 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1834 : 1984 HOT APPLIED SEALING COMPOUNDS FOR JOINTS IN CONCRETE (First Revision)
IS 1834 : 1984.scope: Specifies hot applied sealing compounds intended for use in sealing joints in concrete roads, runways, bridges and other structures. The material covered by this standard is suitable only for longitudinal and transverse joints not more than 12 m apart. The sealing compound shall be employed for filling contraction and construction joints as well as a sealing medium above expansion joint filler to a depth not exceeding 40 mm.

IS 1838 (PART 1) : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1838 (PART 1) : 1983 PREFORMED FILLERS FOR EXPANSION JOINT IN CONCRETE PAVEMENT AND STRUCTURES (NON- EXTRUDING AND RESILIENT TYPE) PART 1 BITUMEN IMPREGNATED FIBRE (First Revision)
IS 1838 (PART 1) : 1983.scope: Specifies the requirements for bitumen impregnated fibre fillers for expansion joints in concrete pavements, runways, and buildings. These preformed strips are designed to be used in expansion joints where non-extruding and resilient type fillers are required.

IS 1838 (Part 2) : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1838 (PART 2) : 1984 PREFORMED FILLERS FOR EXPANSION JOINT IN CONCRETE PAVEMENT AND STRUCTURES (NON-EXTRUDING AND RESILIENT TYPE)
IS 1838 (Part 2) : 1984.scope: Specifies the materials, manufacture, properties and tests for CNSL aldehyde resin and coconut pith based fillers for expansion joints in concrete roads, runways, bridges and other structures.

IS 11433 (PART 1) : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 11433 (PART 1) : 1985 ONE-PART GUN-GRADE POLYSULPHIDE-BASED JOINT SEALANTS
IS 11433 (PART 1) : 1985.scope: This standard covers general requirements for one-part gun-grade polysulphide-based sealants used in sealing or glazing applications in buildings and structures. The sealant must cure at ambient temperature and humidity, and meet specific performance criteria including rheological properties, recovery, mass loss after heat ageing, and adhesion tests.

IS 12118 (Part 1) : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF TWO-PARTS POLYSULPHIDE BASED SEALANTS
IS 12118 (Part 1) : 1987.scope: This standard covers general requirements for two-part polysulphide based sealants, which are used in building applications. It defines two grades: pouring grade for horizontal joints and gun grade for vertical and inclined joints (glazing).

IS 712 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 712 : 1984 BUILDING LIMES (Third Revision)
IS 712 : 1984.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for building limes used in construction. It classifies limes into different classes (A to F) based on their hydraulic properties and intended applications, such as structural use, masonry, plastering, and finishing coats. The standard covers chemical and physical requirements for each class.

IS 2686 : 1977.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2686 : 1977 CINDER AS FINE AGGREGATES FOR USE IN LIME CONCRETE (First Revision)
IS 2686 : 1977.scope: This standard specifies requirements for cinder aggregates used in lime concrete. Cinder aggregates must be well-burnt furnace residue from furnaces using only coal as fuel, clean, and free from clay, dirt, wood ash, or other deleterious matter. The standard defines different classes (A, B, C) for various applications including general purposes, interior work not exposed to damp conditions, and precast blocks.

IS 3068 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3068 : 1986 BROKEN BRICK (BURNT CLAY) COARSE AGGREGATE FOR USE IN LIME CONCRETE
IS 3068 : 1986.scope: This standard specifies requirements for coarse aggregate prepared from broken burnt clay bricks for use in lime concrete. The aggregate must be sourced from well/overburnt bricks conforming to IS 1077:1992 and must be free from underburnt clay particles, soluble salts, and adherent soil or silt. It also outlines physical requirements including grading, bulk density, impact value, water absorption, and soluble matter.

IS 3115 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3115 : 1992 LIME BASED BLOCKS (Second Revision)
IS 3115 : 1992.scope: This standard covers the dimensional, quality, and strength requirements for lime-based blocks, including both hollow and solid types, used in walls, internal partitions, and filler walls. Blocks must be sound, free from cracks and defects, with bedding surfaces at right angles to the face. The ends may be plain, tongued, or double grooved for joint formation.

IS 3182 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3182 : 1986 BROKEN BRICK (BURNED CLAY) FINE AGGREGATE FOR USE IN LIME MORTAR (Second Revision)
IS 3182 : 1986.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for broken brick (burnt clay) fine aggregate intended for use in lime mortar. The aggregate must be prepared from solid bricks conforming to class 50 and above of IS 1077:1992, free from underburnt clay particles, soluble salts, and adherent soil or silt. It outlines physical and chemical requirements for quality control.
keywords: aggregate for use in lime mortar

IS 4098 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4098 : 1983 LIME POZZOLANA MIXTURE (First Revision)
IS 4098 : 1983.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for lime pozzolana mixture used in construction works, including its chemical and physical properties, types for different applications, and delivery specifications. It covers lime pozzolana mixtures for masonry mortars and foundation concrete up to various grades.

IS 4139 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF CALCIUM SILICATE BRICKS (Second Revision)
IS 4139 : 1989.scope: This standard specifies requirements for classification, general quality, dimensions, compressive strength, and drying shrinkage of calcium silicate bricks used in building construction.

IS 10360 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 10360 : 1982 LIME-POZZOLANA CONCRETE BLOCKS FOR PAVING (Second Revision)
IS 10360 : 1982.scope: This standard covers the dimensions, quality, and strength requirements for lime-pozzolana concrete blocks intended for paving applications. The blocks must be sound, free from cracks and defects that interfere with proper placement. They are designed for use in paving where low abrasive resistance is a concern, with a specific construction method involving a cement sand mortar wearing course.

IS 10772 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 10772 :1983 QUICK SETTING LIME POZZOLANA MIXTURE
IS 10772 : 1983.scope: Covers the requirements for lime pozzolana mixtures which tend to set fast for use in construction works except reinforced concrete.

IS 12894 : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12894 : 2002 PULVERIZED FUEL ASH-LIME BRICKS (First Revision)
IS 12894 : 2002.scope: This standard covers the classification, general quality, dimensions, and physical requirements for pulverized fuel ash-lime bricks used in buildings. It specifies the modular sizes, tolerances, and compressive strength classes for these bricks, with reference to higher strength standards for bricks with compressive strength exceeding 30 N/mm².

IS 1127 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1127 : 1970 DIMENSIONS AND WORKMANSHIP OF NATURAL BUILDING STONES FOR MASONRY WORK (First Revision)
IS 1127 : 1970.scope: This standard provides recommendations for the dimensions and workmanship of natural building stones used for various types of stone masonry, including ashlar, block, rubble, and special applications like arches, domes, and copings. It specifies tolerances and dimensional ranges based on masonry type and mortar joint thickness.

IS 1128 : 1974.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1128 : 1974 LIMESTONE (SLAB AND TILES) (First Revision)
IS 1128 : 1974.scope: This standard specifies requirements for dimensions and physical properties of limestone slabs and tiles intended for use in flooring and face work. It covers standards for stone quality, including absence of soft veins, cracks, or flaws, and uniform texture. The standard also defines tolerances for thickness and physical properties such as water absorption, transverse strength, and durability.

IS 1130 : 1969.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1130 : 1969 MARBLE (BLOCKS, SLABS AND TILES)
IS 1130 : 1969.scope: This standard specifies requirements for sizes, physical properties, quality, and workmanship of marble blocks, slabs, and tiles. It covers classification into white and coloured varieties, general requirements for freedom from foreign inclusions and prominent cracks, and dimensional tolerances. The standard also outlines physical properties such as moisture absorption and specific gravity, along with acceptable finishes and size specifications for different product types.

IS 3316 : 1974.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3316 : 1974 STRUCTURAL GRANITE (First Revision)
IS 3316 : 1974.scope: This standard covers the selection, grading, and strength requirements for structural granite used in construction. It specifies requirements for compressive strength, specific gravity, water absorption, and dimensional tolerances for slabs and blocks. Granite must be free from flaws and injurious minerals, and must meet minimum strength and physical property criteria for structural applications.

IS 3620 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3620 : 1979 LATERITE STONE BLOCK FOR MASONRY (First Revision)
IS 3620 : 1979.scope: This standard specifies requirements for rectangular laterite stone blocks used in the construction of walls and partitions. Blocks must be exposed for three months before use, free from soft veins, cracks, cavities, and flaws. Dimensions and workmanship are also defined with tolerance and shape requirements.

IS 3622 : 1977.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3622 : 1977 SANDSTONE (SLABS AND TILES) (First Revision)
IS 3622 : 1977.scope: This standard specifies requirements for dimensions and physical properties of sandstone slabs and tiles used in flooring, roofing, and face work. The stone must be free from soft veins, cracks, and flaws, with uniform texture and colour. Dimensions and tolerances are defined for both rough-cut and machine-cut slabs and tiles.

IS 6250 : 1981.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6250 : 1981 ROOFING SLATE TILES (First Revision)
IS 6250 : 1981.scope: This standard specifies requirements for dimensions, physical properties, and workmanship of slate tiles used for sloped roof covering. It covers the method of laying and fixing of slate tiles as per IS 5119 (Part 1):1968, ensuring uniform color, texture, and absence of defects like veins, cracks, or deleterious minerals.

IS 6579 : 1981.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6579 : 1981 COARSE AGGREGATE FOR WATER BOUND MACADAM (First Revision)
IS 6579 : 1981.scope: Specifies the quality, physical properties and grading of coarse aggregates suitable for use in WBM construction. The aggregates must be hard, durable, and free from excessive flat, elongated, soft or disintegrated particles. They shall be dense, of angular shape, and free from dirt and other similar sources of weakness.

IS 9394 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF STONE LINTELS
IS 9394 : 1979.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for stone lintels made from natural stone, including physical properties, dimensions, tolerances, workmanship, and bearing specifications. It covers the material's durability, strength, and dimensional accuracy for use in building construction. The standard ensures lintels are free from defects and properly installed to support loads.

IS 14223 (PART 1) : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14223 (PART 1) : 1995 POLISHED BUILDING STONES PART1 GRANITE
IS 14223 (PART 1) : 1995.scope: This standard covers the physical properties and finish requirements for polished granite used in building applications. It specifies that granite must be free from imperfections and injurious minerals that may affect appearance, integrity, or polishing characteristics. The standard also details dimensional tolerances and surface finish requirements for the stone slabs.

IS 1077 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1077 : 1992 COMMON BURNT CLAY BUILDING BRICKS (Fifth Revision)
IS 1077 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for classification, general quality, dimensions, and physical properties of common burnt clay building bricks used in buildings with compressive strength less than 40 N/mm². It covers modular and non-modular bricks, including specifications for frog size, dimensions, and tolerances. Higher-strength bricks are referenced to another standard.

IS 2180 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2180 : 1988 HEAVY DUTY BURNT CLAY BUILDING BRICKS (Third Revision)
IS 2180 : 1988.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for classification, general quality, dimensions, and physical properties of heavy duty burnt clay building bricks. Bricks shall be manufactured by pressing or extrusion and must meet specified compressive strength, water absorption, efflorescence, and bulk density criteria.

IS 2222 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2222 : 1991 BURNT CLAY PERFORATED BUILDING BRICKS (Third Revision)
IS 2222 : 1991.scope: This standard covers the dimensions, quality, and physical requirements of perforated burnt clay bricks for use in walls and partitions. The bricks shall be free from cracks, flaws, and nodules of free lime, with a sharp straight edge at right angle and uniform colour and texture. It specifies standard sizes, tolerances, and performance criteria for both modular and non-modular bricks.

IS 2691 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF BURNT CLAY FACING BRICKS (Second Revision)
IS 2691 : 1988.scope: Specifies the dimensions, quality and strength of burnt clay facing bricks used in buildings and other structures. The bricks shall be of uniform colour, free from cracks, flaws and nodules of free lime, and have plane rectangular faces with parallel sides and sharp straight right-angled edges.

IS 3583 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3583 : 1988 BURNT CLAY PAVING BRICKS (Second Revision)
IS 3583 : 1988.scope: Covers dimensions, quality and strength, and methods of sampling and test for burnt clay paving bricks for use in construction of pavements. Bricks shall be mechanically shaped, not hand moulded, with uniformly dense structure, free from lime, large voids, and marked laminations, and shall have smooth rectangular faces and sharp corners. Bricks shall not be provided with frogs.

IS 3952 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3952 : 1988 BURNT CLAY HOLLOW BRICKS FOR WALLS AND PARTITIONS (Second Revision)
IS 3952 : 1988.scope: Covers the dimensions, quality and strength requirements of hollow bricks made from burnt clay and having perforations through and at right angle to the bearing surface.

IS 4885 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4885 : 1988 SEWER BRICKS (First Revision)
IS 4885 : 1988.scope: Specifies dimensions, quality and strength, and methods of sampling and test for burnt clay sewer bricks used for sanitary sewage.

IS 5779 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5779 : 1986 BURNT CLAY SOLING BRICKS (First Revision)
IS 5779 : 1986.scope: This standard specifies requirements for burnt clay soling bricks used in road construction, including dimensions, general quality, and physical properties such as compressive strength, water absorption, and efflorescence.

IS 6165 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF DIMENSIONS FOR SPECIAL SHAPES OF CLAY BRICKS
IS 6165 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies dimensions for special shapes of clay bricks used in building and other civil engineering construction. It conforms to IS 1077:1991* and IS 2180:1988† for modular and non-modular bricks.

IS 13757 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13757 : 1993 BURNT CLAY FLY ASH BUILDING BRICKS
IS 13757 : 1993.scope: This standard covers the requirements for classification, general quality, dimensions, and physical properties of common burnt clay fly ash building bricks used in buildings. It specifies compressive strength classes and includes notes on alternative standards for higher strength bricks.

IS 7556 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 7556 : 1988 BURNT CLAY JALLIES (First Revision)
IS 7556 : 1988.scope: This standard covers dimensions, quality, and strength requirements of burnt clay jallies used for ornamental designs, such as screens on verandahs, parapet construction, and boundary walls.

IS 654 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 654 : 1992 CLAY ROOFING TILES, MANGALORE PATTERN (Third Revision)
IS 654 : 1992.scope: Covers the machine-pressed clay interlocking roofing tiles of the 'Mangalore Pattern'. These tiles are classified into Class AA and Class A based on their water absorption and breaking load characteristics. The standard specifies requirements for shape, lugs, dimensions, weight, strength, permeability, and tie-down hole specifications.

IS 1464 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1464 : 1992 CLAY RIDGE AND CEILING TILES (Second Revision)
IS 1464 : 1992.scope: Covers machine pressed clay ridge and ceiling tiles. It does not cover tiles of irregular sizes, shapes and colour, and those made to meet special requirements. The tiles shall be uniform in shape, free from irregularities, and shall not have cracks, bends, or lamination.

IS 1478 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1478 : 1992 CLAY FLOORING TILES (Second Revision)
IS 1478 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies requirements for dimensions, quality, and strength of clay flooring tiles. It classifies tiles into three classes based on water absorption and flexural strength. The standard also outlines dimensional tolerances, warpage limits, and general quality criteria for tile faces and edges.

IS 2690 (PART 1) : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2690 (PART 1) : 1993 BURNT CLAY-FLAT TERRACING TILES PART 1 MACHINE-MADE (Second Revision)
IS 2690 (PART 1) : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements for machine-made burnt clay flat terracing tiles, including dimensions, tolerances, general quality, warpage, water absorption, and flexural strength. It ensures uniformity in shape and size, freedom from irregularities, and meets minimum strength and durability criteria for use in terracing applications.

IS 2690 (PART 2) : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2690 (PART 2) : 1992 BURNT CLAY FLAT TERRACING TILES PART 2 HAND - MADE (Second Revision)
IS 2690 (PART 2) : 1992.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hand-made burnt clay flat terracing tiles. It covers dimensions, tolerances, warpage, water absorption, and flexural strength. The tiles must be uniform in shape and size, free from irregularities, and meet specified mechanical and dimensional criteria.

IS 3367 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3367 : 1993 BURNT CLAY TILES FOR USE IN LINING IRRIGATION AND DRAINAGE WORKS (Second Revision)
IS 3367 : 1993.scope: Covers machine-pressed, wire-cut, or hand-made rectangular burnt clay tiles used for lining irrigation canals and drainage channels (other than sewage works).

IS 3951 (PART 1) : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3951 (PART 1) : 1975 HOLLOW CLAY TILES FOR FLOORS AND ROOFS
IS 3951 (PART 1) : 1975.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hollow clay tiles intended for use in floors and roofs, including dimensions, quality, and strength specifications. The tiles must be free from cracks, flaws, and nodules of free lime, with uniform color and rectangular faces. They are designed to have perforations parallel to their length and must meet dimensional tolerances and mechanical strength criteria.

IS 3951 (PART 2) : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3951 (PART 2) : 1975 HOLLOW CLAY TILES FOR FLOORS AND ROOFS PART 2 STRUCTURAL TYPE (First Revision)
IS 3951 (PART 2) : 1975.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for structural hollow clay tiles suitable for floors and roofs, covering quality, dimensions, bulk density, water absorption, and strength. The tiles must be free from cracks, flaws, and deleterious materials, with specific dimensional tolerances and mechanical properties.

IS 2095 (PART 1) : 1996.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2095 (PART 1) : 1996 GYPSUM PLASTER BOARDS PART 1 PLAIN GYPSUM PLASTER BOARDS (Second Revision)
IS 2095 (PART 1) : 1996.scope: This standard covers requirements for gypsum plaster boards intended for use as vertical or horizontal lining in buildings, including for direct surface decoration or gypsum plaster finishes. The boards are classified by their use, such as wallboards, baseboards, and specialized types for high-temperature applications.

IS 2095 (PART 3) : 1996.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2095 (PART 3) : 1996 GYPSUM PLASTER BOARDS PART 3 REINFORCED GYPSUM PLASTER BOARDS (Second Revision)
IS 2095 (PART 3) : 1996.scope: Covers the method of manufacture, tests and sampling of fibrous gypsum plaster boards and glass fibre reinforced gypsum (GRG) boards for use as a lining material for ceiling, dry surfacing material for walls, door panels or for partitions.

IS 2547 (PART 1) : 1976.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2547 (PART 1) : 1976 GYPSUM BUILDING PLASTERS PART 1 EXCLUDING PREMIXED LIGHTWEIGHT PLASTERS (First Revision)
IS 2547 (PART 1) : 1976.scope: Covers the classification and chemical and physical requirements for gypsum building plasters which possess a definite set due to hydration of calcium sulphate, anhydrous or hemihydrate, to form gypsum products. Premixed lightweight building plasters are not included.

IS 2547 (PART 2) : 1976.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2547 (PART 2) : 1976 GYPSUM BUILDING PLASTER PART 2 PREMIXED LIGHTWEIGHT PLASTERS (First Revision)
IS 2547 (PART 2) : 1976.scope: This standard covers requirements for premixed lightweight plasters consisting essentially of gypsum plaster and lightweight aggregate used in general building operations. It classifies plasters into undercoat and final coat types, specifying physical and chemical properties for each. The standard also details properties such as dry bulk density, compressive strength, and free lime content for different plaster types.

IS 2849 : 1983.title: NON-LOAD BEARING GYPSUM PARTITION BLOCKS (SOLID AND HOLLOW TYPES)
IS 2849 : 1983.scope: This standard specifies requirements for gypsum partition blocks used in non-load bearing construction for interior buildings and for protecting columns, elevator shafts, and against fire. The blocks may be solid or hollow types with true rectangular shapes and straight edges.

IS 8272 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8272 : 1984 GYPSUM PLASTER FOR USE IN THE MANUFACTURE OF FIBROUS PLASTER BOARDS (First Revision)
IS 8272 : 1984.scope: This standard covers the requirements and methods of sampling and testing for calcined gypsum plaster used in manufacturing fibrous plaster boards. Gypsum plasters are used extensively in general building operations and for manufacturing preformed gypsum building products, which have advantages of lightness and high fire resistance. Fibrous plaster boards are used as coverings for walls, ceilings, and partitions in normally dry environments.

IS 399 : 1963.title: SUMMARY OF IS 399 : 1963 CLASSIFICATION OF COMMERCIAL TIMBERS AND THEIR ZONAL DISTRIBUTION (Revised)
IS 399 : 1963.scope: This standard provides a summary of the zonal distribution of common commercial timbers in India, classified according to their uses and availability. It includes information on the availability, weight, durability, treatability, refractoriness to air seasoning, and comparative strength coefficients of timbers across five defined zones.

IS 12896 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF INDIAN TIMBERS FOR DOOR AND WINDOW SHUTTERS AND FRAMES – CLASSIFICATION
IS 12896 : 1990.scope: This standard covers the general classification of Indian timber species suitable for door and window shutters and frames. It lays down general requirements for quality, seasoning, moisture content, and preservative treatments. The standard does not cover species suitable for flush doors.

IS 190 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF CONIFEROUS SAWN TIMBER (BAULKS AND SCANTLINGS) (Fourth Revision)
IS 190 : 1991.scope: Covers the requirements of coniferous sawn timber (baulks and scantlings).

IS 876 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 876 : 1992 WOOD POLES FOR OVER HEAD POWER AND TELECOMMUNICATION LINES (Third Revision)
IS 876 : 1992.scope: Covers wood poles made of both broad leaved and coniferous species of timber suitable for carrying overhead electric power transmission lines, telephone and telegraph circuits.

IS 1326 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1326 : 1992 NON-CONIFEROUS SAWN TIMBER (BAULKS AND SCANTLING) (Second Revision)
IS 1326 : 1992.scope: Covers the requirements of non-coniferous sawn timber in the form of baulks and scantling. It specifies dimensions, grading, and defects for structural use. The standard also includes provisions for end coatings and treatment to prevent cracking and splitting.

IS 2372 : 2004.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2372 : 2004 TIMBER FOR COOLING TOWERS (First Revision)
IS 2372 : 2004.scope: Covers the species, grades, requirements and treatments for timber used in the construction of cooling towers. The species of timber suitable for cooling towers shall be as given in Table 1. Grading of timber shall be of three grades, namely select grade, Grade I and Grade II depending on the defects permitted.

IS 3337 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3337 : 1978 BALLIES FOR GENERAL PURPOSES (First Revision)
IS 3337 : 1978.scope: This standard covers the requirements for ballies (timber products) used for general purposes. It specifies the species of timber suitable, manufacturing process, dimensions, moisture content, and permissible defects. The standard also outlines preservation methods and measurement criteria for these timber products.

IS 3629 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF STRUCTURAL TIMBER IN BUILDINGS (First Revision)
IS 3629 : 1986.scope: Covers the various requirements of structural timber for use in buildings. It includes classification and grouping of different species of timber, their suitability for permanent and temporary structures, factors affecting strength, tolerances on dimensions, and influence of defects.

IS 3731 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF TEAK SQUARES (First Revision)
IS 3731 : 1985.scope: Covers the requirements of various grades of teak squares based on defects. Teak squares shall be of good sound wood and free from defects other than those permitted. All pieces shall be air-seasoned to a moisture content not exceeding 20 percent.

IS 4895 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4895 : 1985 TEAK LOGS (First Revision)
IS 4895 : 1985.scope: Covers the requirements of various grades of teak logs intended for conversion purposes. It does not cover the requirements of teak logs for veneering purposes. Logs shall be free from hollow heart, shatter, any kind of decay (rot), and live insect attack.

IS 5246 : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF CONIFEROUS LOGS (First revision)
IS 5246 : 2000.scope: Covers the requirements of three grades of coniferous logs for conversion into timber. The logs shall be free from hollow centre above 15 percent of the basal area and shall be of specified species. Permissible defects are listed, including lack of straightness, taper, end splits, surface cracks, cup shakes, knots, hollow centre, and wounds, with specific limits for each grade.

IS 6056 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF JOINTED WOOD POLES FOR OVERHEAD POWER TELECOMMUNICATION LINES
IS 6056 : 1970.scope: Covers the specification of jointed wood poles made of broad leaved and coniferous species grown in India, suitable for carrying overhead electric power transmission lines and telephone circuits.

IS 7308 : 1999.title: SUMMARY OF NON-CONIFEROUS LOGS (First Revision)
IS 7308 : 1999.scope: This standard covers the requirements for non-coniferous logs intended for conversion into sawn timber, specifying grades, dimensions, and permissible defects. It defines limits on defects per log length and outlines acceptable characteristics for logs to ensure suitability for timber production.

IS 10394 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 10394 : 1982 WOODEN SLEEPERS FOR RAILWAY TRACK
IS 10394 : 1982.scope: Covers the requirements of wooden sleepers and wooden specials used for broad gauge and narrow gauge railway tracks. It includes specifications for dimensions, tolerances, timber species, preservative treatment, grading, and permissible defects.

IS 73 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF PAVING BITUMEN (Second Revision)
IS 73 : 1992.scope: Covers physical and chemical requirements of paving bitumens for use in roadways, runways and allied constructions.

IS 212 : 1983.title: CRUDE COAL TAR FOR GENERAL USE
IS 212 : 1983.scope: Covers the requirements of crude coal tar used for general purposes, such as treatment of wooden poles and sleepers, toilet walls, fishing nets, etc. Shall be obtained as a by-product of destructive distillation of coal.

IS 215 : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF IS 215 : 1995 ROAD TAR (Third Revision)
IS 215 : 1995.scope: Covers two types of tar, each with five grades, suitable for different road construction conditions under various climatic conditions in the country. The tar is used for surface dressing, dense tarsurfacing, open graded premix carpet, and various road construction applications including priming, surface painting, renewal coats, and waterproofing.

IS 216 : 1961.title: SUMMARY OF IS 216 : 1961 COAL TAR PITCH (Revised)
IS 216 : 1961.scope: This standard specifies requirements for four grades of coal tar pitch with softening points ranging from 45°C to 92°C, intended for use in waterproofing, protective and binding compounds for masonry, timber, and concrete structures, as well as for caulking decks, carbon electrodes, coal briquettes, damp-proof courses, flooring mastics, and as a base for coal tar paints.

IS 218 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 218 : 1983 CREOSOTE OIL FOR USE AS WOOD PRESERVATIVES
IS 218 : 1983.scope: Covers materials commercially known as coal tar creosote oil primarily used for preservation of wood. It shall be homogeneous liquid and consist essentially of distillate of coal tar. The materials shall also comply with the requirements prescribed in Table 1.

IS 454 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF CUTBACK BITUMEN FROM WAXY CRUDE (Second Revision)
IS 454 : 1994.scope: Covers the physical and chemical requirements of cutbacks bitumen from waxy crude of indigenous origin. Used for surface dressing and resurfacing operations, and for pre-mix type of construction.

IS 3117 : 2004.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3117 : 2004 BITUMEN EMULSION FOR ROADS AND ALLIED APPLICATIONS (ANIONIC TYPE) (First Revision)
IS 3117 : 2004.scope: This standard specifies the physical and chemical requirements for anionic type bitumen emulsion used in road construction and allied applications. It defines different types (Rapid, Medium, Slow Setting) based on setting times and aggregate compatibility, and outlines material specifications for bitumen and emulsifying agents.

IS 8887 : 2004.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8887 : 2004 BITUMEN EMULSION FOR ROADS (CATIONIC TYPE) (Second Revision)
IS 8887 : 2004.scope: Covers the physical and chemical requirements of bitumen emulsions (cationic type) for roads. The standard defines grades such as Rapid setting -1, Rapid setting -2, Medium setting, and Show setting, with specific requirements for stability, coagulation, and coating ability.

IS 1237 : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF CEMENT CONCRETE FLOORING TILES (Second Revision)
IS 1237 : 1980.scope: This standard specifies requirements for cement concrete flooring tiles, including plain, plain coloured, and terrazzo types. It covers classification, dimensions, thickness, tolerances, and physical properties for general and heavy-duty applications such as office buildings, schools, hospitals, residential buildings, footpaths, and staircases.

IS 1542 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF SAND FOR PLASTER (Second Revision)
IS 1542 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for naturally occurring sands and crushed gravel sands used in internal wall and ceiling plastering, and external plastering using lime, cement, composite lime-cement, or gypsum mixes. It covers the quality, grading, and deleterious materials for sand used in plastering applications.

IS 4457 : 1982.title: CERAMIC UNGLAZED VITREOUS ACID RESISTING TILES
IS 4457 : 1982.scope: This standard specifies requirements for ceramic unglazed vitreous acid resisting tiles, including dimensions, tolerances, thickness, and performance characteristics such as water absorption, compressive strength, and abrasion resistance. It covers sizes like 100×100 mm, 150×150 mm, and 200×200 mm, with specific tolerances and testing methods for quality assurance.

IS 4832 (PART 1) : 1969.title: SUMMARY OF CHEMICAL RESISTANT MORTARS PART I — SILICATE TYPE
IS 4832 (PART 1) : 1969.scope: This standard covers requirements for chemically setting silicate type mortar units used for bonding masonry, which are resistant to most acids except hydrofluoric acid and concentrated orthophosphoric acids. They are not resistant to alkalis or boiling water and deteriorate with continued exposure to water.

IS 4832 (PART 2) : 1969.title: SUMMARY OF CHEMICAL RESISTANT MORTARS PART 2 RESIN TYPE
IS 4832 (PART 2) : 1969.scope: This standard specifies requirements for resin type chemical resistant mortars used for bonding acid-proof bricks and tiles. These mortars have good resistance to non-oxidizing mineral acid and poor resistance to oxidizing alkalis, and are resistant to water to give impermeable joints. The standard covers materials, physical requirements, chemical resistance, and shelf life.

IS 4832 (PART 3) : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF CHEMICAL RESISTANT MORTARS PART 3 – SULPHUR TYPE
IS 4832 (PART 3) : 1968.scope: This standard specifies requirements for sulphur type chemical resistant mortars used for bonding acid resistance bricks or tiles. These mortars have good resistance against most acids except concentrated oxidizing acids but poor resistance to alkalis. They are suitable for applications requiring chemical resistance in masonry units.

IS 4860 : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF ACID - RESISTANT BRICKS
IS 4860 : 1968.scope: This standard covers requirements for acid-resistant bricks designed for use in chemical allied industries and areas subject to acid attack, such as masonry, flooring, and lining of sewers carrying industrial effluents. These bricks are made from suitable clay or shale with low lime and iron content, vitrified at high temperatures.

IS 13753 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13753 : 1993 DUST-PRESSED CERAMIC TILES WITH WATER ABSORPTION OF E>10% GROUP B III
IS 13753 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements for dust-pressed ceramic glazed tiles with water absorption greater than 10% (Group B III) for use in wall and floor coverings. These tiles are intended for areas not subject to severe mechanical loads or frost conditions.

IS 13754 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13754 : 1993 DUST-PRESSED CERAMIC TILES WITH WATER ABSORPTION OF 6% < E ≤ 10% (GROUP B II B)
IS 13754 : 1993.scope: Specifies the sizes, dimensional tolerances, mechanical, physical and chemical requirements of ceramic tiles. It is applicable only to dust-pressed ceramic tiles of first quality, including tiles premounted on sheets with a water absorption of 6%<E≤10% for interior and exterior use on both floors and walls.

IS 13755 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13755 : 1993 DUST-PRESSED CERAMIC TILES WITH WATER ABSORPTION OF 3% < E ≤ 6% (GROUP – B II A)
IS 13755 : 1993.scope: Specifies the sizes, dimensional tolerances, mechanical, physical and chemical requirements, surface quality requirements and marking of ceramic tiles. It is applicable only to dust-pressed ceramic tiles of first quality, including tiles premounted on sheets, with a water absorption of 3%<E≤6% according to Group B IIa of IS 13712:1993 for interior and exterior use on both floors and walls.

IS 13756 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13756 : 1993 DUST-PRESSED CERAMIC TILES WITH LOW WATER ABSORPTION OF E ≤ 3% GROUP B1
IS 13756 : 1993.scope: Specifies the sizes, dimensional tolerances, mechanical, physical and chemical requirements, surface quality requirements and marking of ceramic tiles. It is applicable only to dust-pressed ceramic tiles including tiles premounted on sheets of first quality with a low water absorption (E≤3%) according to Group B1 of IS 13712:1993 Ceramic tiles—Classifications, Characteristics and marking. For interior use on both floors and walls.

IS 14862 : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF FIBRE CEMENT FLAT SHEETS
IS 14862 : 2000.scope: This standard covers the characteristics and methods of control and test for fibre cement flat sheets intended for external applications such as cladding facades, curtain walls, and soffits, and for internal use such as partitions, floors, and ceiling. These sheets may have either a smooth or textured surface.

IS 14871 : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF PRODUCTS IN FIBRE REINFORCED CEMENT—LONG CORRUGATED OR ASYMMETRICAL SECTION SHEETS AND FITTINGS FOR ROOFING AND CLADDING
IS 14871 : 2000.scope: This standard covers the requirement for straight fibre cement profiled sheets and their fittings used for roofing and cladding. It also specifies tests for checking these characteristics and conditions for acceptance.

IS 1195 : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1195 : 2002 BITUMEN MASTIC FOR FLOORING (Second Revision)
IS 1195 : 2002.scope: This standard specifies requirements for four grades of bitumen mastic for flooring, composed of ground limestone, coarse aggregate, and pigment. It covers special grades for special purposes like chemical resistant, oil resistant, or spark free flooring, and provides guidance on selection in Appendix A.

IS 5317 : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5317 : 2002 BITUMEN MASTIC FOR BRIDGE DECKING AND ROADS (Second Revision)
IS 5317 : 2002.scope: This standard specifies requirements for bitumen mastic used as a surfacing material for bridge decks and roads. It covers physical properties, composition, and aggregate specifications for bitumen mastic. The standard is intended for use in road and bridge construction applications.

IS 8374 : 1977.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8374 : 1977 BITUMEN MASTIC, ANTI-STATIC AND ELECTRICALLY CONDUCTING GRADE
IS 8374 : 1977.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for bitumen mastic of anti-static and electrically conducting grades, intended for use in locations where precautionary measures against static electricity accumulation are necessary to ensure safety. It covers the composition, physical properties, and grading of materials used in its preparation.

IS 13026 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13026 : 1991 BITUMEN MASTIC FOR FLOORING FOR INDUSTRIES HANDLING LPG AND OTHER LIGHT HYDROCARBON PRODUCTS
IS 13026 : 1991.scope: This standard specifies requirements for bitumen mastic flooring used in industries handling LPG and other light hydrocarbon products. It is also applicable to explosive and crackers manufacturing factories, ordnance factories, and ammonia depots. The standard is not applicable for less volatile materials such as kerosene, diesel, and lubricating oil.

IS 653 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF LINOLEUM SHEET AND TILES (Third Revision)
IS 653 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for linoleum manufactured as sheets or tiles on a hessian backing and used as floor covering. It covers materials, types, dimensions, physical properties, and packing for linoleum sheets, tiles, and cork tiles.

IS 3461 : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF PVC ASBESTOS FLOOR TILES (First Revision)
IS 3461 : 1980.scope: This standard covers requirements for smooth surfaced homogeneous PVC asbestos floor tiles and laminated floor tiles with embossed surfaces. It specifies materials, dimensions, tolerances, and physical properties such as squareness, dimensional stability, colour fastness, and impact resistance. The standard is intended for manufacturers and buyers to ensure quality and consistency in PVC asbestos floor tiles.

IS 3462 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF UNBACKED FLEXIBLE PVC FLOORING (Second Revision)
IS 3462 : 1986.scope: This standard specifies requirements for unbacked flexible PVC flooring, including laminated and non-laminated types, which may be supplied in continuous lengths or tile form. The flooring consists of a thermoplastic binder blended with vinyl chloride polymer or copolymer, along with fillers, pigments, plasticizers, and stabilizers. It covers dimensions, thickness, color, surface characteristics, and performance tests.

IS 9197 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9197 : 1979 EPOXY RESIN, HARDENERS AND EPOXY RESIN COMPOSITIONS FOR FLOOR TOPPING
IS 9197 : 1979.scope: This standard lays down the requirements for epoxy resin, hardeners, and other constituent materials used in the formulation of floor toppings. It specifies the properties of epoxy resin compositions, including grades, and details requirements for hardeners, liquid coal tar, and aggregates. The standard also covers the properties of the final epoxy resin-hardener-filler mixture.

IS 12866 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12866 : 1989 PLASTIC TRANSLUCENT SHEET MADE FROM THERMOSSETTING POLYESTER RESIN (GLASS FIBRE REINFORCED)
IS 12866 : 1989.scope: Specifies dimensions, tolerances, strength and light transmission of glass fibre reinforced translucent plastic sheeting for use in roofs, conforming to broad specifications given in IS 277:1992, IS 459:1992, and IS 1254:1991.

IS 638 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 638 : 1979 SHEET RUBBER JOINTING AND RUBBER INSERTION JOINTING (Second Revision)
IS 638 : 1979.scope: This standard specifies requirements and methods for sheet rubber jointing and rubber insertion jointing used in flanges and similar joints subjected to water, air, or low pressure steam. It covers materials, construction, and performance criteria for joints under various pressure conditions.

IS 809 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 809 : 1992 RUBBER FLOORING MATERIALS FOR GENERAL PURPOSE (First Revision)
IS 809 : 1992.scope: This standard lays down the composition, minimum requirements, workmanship and prescribes tests for rubber flooring material suitable for covering floors of domestic and public buildings, cinemas, hospitals, large stores, ships, and transport vehicles. It does not cover requirements for special types of rubber flooring used for electrical insulating purposes, conductive or antistatic flooring, or rubber flooring having chemical and oil-resistant properties.

IS 1322 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1322 : 1993 BITUMEN FELTS FOR WATER-PROOFING AND DAMP-PROOFING (Fourth Revision)
IS 1322 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements for saturated and self-finished bitumen felts used for water-proofing and damp-proofing applications. It covers classification, minimum weights, dimensions, and various performance tests for different types and grades of felts.

IS 1580 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF BITUMINOUS COMPOUNDS FOR WATER-PROOFING AND CAULKING PURPOSES (Second Revision)
IS 1580 : 1991.scope: This standard covers bituminous compounds used for stopping leaks through cracks in roofs, floors, and walls, as sealants for plate joints of wagons, coaches, and buses, as caulking agents for crevices and vertical joints between steel plates, folded sections, and wood joints, and as adhesives for rainguards for rubber trees.

IS 2645 : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2645 : 2003 INTEGRAL CEMENT WATER-PROOFING COMPOUNDS (Second Revision)
IS 2645 : 2003.scope: This standard specifies requirements for integral cement waterproofing compounds, including permeability to water, setting time, and compressive strength tests. It covers compounds mixed with cement and sand, ensuring performance comparable to ordinary Portland cement under specified conditions.

IS 3037 : 1986.title: BITUMEN MASTIC FOR USE IN WATER-PROOFING OF ROOFS
IS 3037 : 1986.scope: This standard specifies requirements for bitumen mastic suitable for water-proofing of roofs. It is not intended for use as a paving material or under exceptional conditions such as acid or alkali actions. The mastic shall consist of a mixture of bitumen, aggregates, and mineral filler to give a semi-fluid consistency when heated to about 180°C, allowing it to be easily compressible by trowels into a compact and uniform layer not less than 10 mm in thickness.

IS 3384 : 1986.title: BITUMEN PRIMER FOR USE IN WATER-PROOFING AND DAMP-PROOFING
IS 3384 : 1986.scope: Covers the requirements for bitumen primer for application to concrete and masonry surfaces and to be used with bitumen in damp-proofing and waterproofing below or above ground level.

IS 5871 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF BITUMEN MASTIC FOR TANKING AND DAMP-PROOFING (First Revision)
IS 5871 : 1987.scope: This standard specifies requirements for bitumen mastic used as a covering material for damp-proofing underground tanks, basements, swimming pools, irrigation canals, and water reservoirs. It details the composition, physical properties, and grading of aggregates and fillers to ensure proper consistency and performance when heated.

IS 7193 : 1974.title: SUMMARY OF GLASS FIBRE BASE BITUMEN FELTS (First Revision)
IS 7193 : 1974.scope: This standard covers requirements for self-finished glass fibre bitumen felts used in waterproofing and damp-proofing applications in buildings and other structures where moisture penetration must be stopped. The felts are classified into Grade I and Grade II based on their surface treatment and intended use.

IS 12027 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF SILICONE-BASED WATER REPELLENTS
IS 12027 : 1987.scope: This standard specifies requirements for silicone-based water repellents applicable to masonry generally free from cracks exceeding 0.10 mm in width, to confer water repellency without appreciable change of colour or appearance other than that imparted by fugitive dye.

IS 14695 : 1999.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14695 : 1999 GLASS FIBRE BASE COAL TAR PITCH OUTER WRAP
IS 14695 : 1999.scope: Covers the requirement for glass fibre base coal tar pitch outerwrap used for corrosion protection of buried mild steel pipelines.

IS 775 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF IS 775 : 1970 CAST IRON BRACKETS AND SUPPORTS FOR WASH-BASINS AND SINKS (Second Revision)
IS 775 : 1970.scope: This standard covers requirements for material, construction, workmanship, dimensions, weights, and finish of cast iron brackets and supports for wash basins and sinks. It specifies types and weights of various bracket and support systems, including cantilever, recessed, wall fixing, and strap and leg supports. It also outlines construction details and finish options for these components.

IS 782 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 782 : 1978 CAULKING LEAD (Third Revision)
IS 782 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for different types of caulking lead suitable for use in water supply and sanitary installations, including pig lead, lead wool, and lead yarn, each designed for specific joint types such as gas, water, and sewer lines where cast lead is impractical.

IS 804 : 1967.title: SUMMARY OF IS 804 : 1967 RECTANGULAR PRESSED STEEL TANKS (First Revision)
IS 804 : 1967.scope: This standard specifies requirements for materials, fabrication, erection, and supply of rectangular pressed steel tanks used for storing cold and hot water and certain other liquids under pressure not greater than the static head corresponding to the depth of the tank. It does not cover tanks storing liquids at temperatures higher than 100°C or those subject to earth or other external pressure besides wind pressure.

IS 1700 : 1973.title: SUMMARY OF DRINKING FOUNTAINS (First Revision)
IS 1700 : 1973.scope: Covers the material, construction, essential hygienic and performance requirements and finish of drinking fountains used in schools, parks and other public places.

IS 2963 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2963 : 1979 COPPER ALLOY WASTE FITTINGS FOR WASH-BASINS AND SINKS (First Revision)
IS 2963 : 1979.scope: This standard specifies requirements for materials, manufacture, and workmanship of copper alloy waste fittings used in wash-basins and sinks, ensuring compliance with prescribed standards.

IS 3489 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF ENAMELLED STEEL BATH TUBS
IS 3489 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the material, construction, patterns, dimensions, tolerances, and maintenance of vitreous enameled steel bath tubs. It covers the design and manufacturing specifications for these bathroom fixtures, including their structural integrity and surface finish.

IS 5219 : 1969.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5219 : 1969 CAST COPPER ALLOYS TRAPS PART 1 AND STRAPS
IS 5219 : 1969.scope: This standard covers copper alloy cast traps and their associated components for use in wash-basins, sinks, bath tubs, and similar waste appliances. It specifies materials, workmanship, design, and dimensions for 'P' and 'S' type traps and their parts.

IS 8718 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF VITREOUS ENAMELLED STEEL KITCHEN SINKS
IS 8718 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for vitreous enamelled steel kitchen sinks, covering material, construction, patterns, sizes, tolerances, surface coating, finish, abrasion resistance, alkali resistance, and thickness specifications. It ensures uniform quality, durability, and visual satisfaction for kitchen sinks made from mild steel sheets with enamel coating.

IS 8727 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8727 : 1978 VITREOUS ENAMELLED STEEL WASH-BASINS
IS 8727 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for vitreous enamelled steel wash basins, covering material, construction, workmanship, patterns, sizes, tolerances, and marking. It defines specifications for mild steel sheets and includes dimensional tolerances, surface coating, and defect limits. The standard ensures durability through abrasion, alkali, and acid resistance tests.

IS 12701 : 1996.title: SUMMARY OF ROTATIONAL MOULDED POLYETHYLENE WATER STORAGE TANKS (First Revision)
IS 12701 : 1996.scope: This standard specifies requirements for rotational moulded polyethylene water storage tanks, including materials, dimensions, construction, and performance. It covers cylindrical and rectangular vertical tanks designed for above-ground use with a nominal service temperature of +1°C to +50°C. The tanks are not intended for underground applications.

IS 13983 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13983 : 1994 STAINLESS STEEL SINKS FOR DOMESTIC PURPOSES
IS 13983 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements for stainless steel sinks used in domestic settings, covering material, construction, dimensions, and workmanship. It defines types of sinks (single or double bowl, with or without drainer) and details specifications for tap holes, overflow holes, and sink bowl dimensions. The standard does not specify methods of fixing or sealing.

IS 407 : 1981.title: SUMMARY OF IS 407 : 1981 BRASS TUBES FOR GENERAL PURPOSES (Third revision)
IS 407 : 1981.scope: Specifies the method of designating solid drawn brass tubes for general purposes, detailing permitted tolerances on outside diameter, thickness, and length. It covers requirements for freedom from defects, condition (temper), sizes, and physical tests.

IS 2501 : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2501 : 1995 SOLID DRAWN COPPER TUBES FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES (Third Revision)
IS 2501 : 1995.scope: This standard specifies requirements for solid drawn (seamless) copper tubes intended for general engineering purposes. It covers different grades such as Cu-ETP, Cu-DHP, Cu-FRTP, Cu-DPA, and Cu-ATP, with various supply conditions including as drawn, half hard, and annealed. The tubes must meet dimensional tolerances, freedom from defects, and physical property specifications.

IS 1230 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1230 : 1979 CAST IRON RAINWATER PIPES AND FITTINGS (Second Revision)
IS 1230 : 1979.scope: This standard covers requirements for cast iron rainwater pipes, half-round gutters, their fittings, and accessories. It specifies dimensions, mass, tolerances, and tests for soundness and hardness. Appendix A details requirements for O.G. gutters and fittings.
keywords: cast iron rainwater pipes and fittings

IS 1536 : 2001.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1536 : 2001 CENTRIFUGALLY CAST (SPUN) IRON PRESSURE PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE (Fourth Revision)
IS 1536 : 2001.scope: This standard covers centrifugally cast spun iron pipes for water, gas, and sewage applications. It specifies requirements for pipes manufactured in metal or sand-lined moulds, including flanged and socket/spigot joints. The standard ensures safety and interchangeability through defined dimensions, mechanical tests, and hydrostatic pressure tests.

IS 1537 : 1976.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1537 : 1976 VERTICALLY CAST IRON PRESSURE PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE
IS 1537 : 1976.scope: This standard covers requirements for cast iron pipes manufactured by vertical casing in sand moulds for water, gas, and sewage applications. It applies to pipes with sockets or flanges and may be extended to other joint types like rubber joints to ensure interchangeability.

IS 1538 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1538 : 1993 CAST IRON FITTINGS FOR PRESSURE PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE (Third Revision)
IS 1538 : 1993.scope: This standard covers general requirements for cast iron fittings for pressure pipes used in water, gas, and sewage systems. It applies to all cast iron fittings with spigots, sockets, or flanges, specifying dimensions and material quality, excluding joint-specific details.

IS 2002 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2002 : 1979 SAND CAST IRON SPIGOT AND SOCKET SOIL, WASTE AND VENTILATING PIPES, FITTINGS AND ACCESSORIES (Second Revision)
IS 2002 : 1979.scope: This standard covers sand cast iron spigot and socket soil, waste and ventilating pipes, fittings, and accessories. These products are suitable for use above ground only. They must meet specific requirements for defects, sound when struck, hydraulic pressure tests, and dimensional tolerances.

IS 1879 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1879 : 1987 MALLEABLE CAST IRON PIPE FITTINGS (Second Revision)
IS 1879 : 1987.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for malleable cast iron pipe fittings, including elbows, tees, crosses, bends, sockets, bushings, hexagon nipples, unions, caps, and plugs. These fittings are threaded in accordance with IS 554:1985 and are designed for the transmission of fluid, steam, air, and oil up to specified pressure limits. The fittings shall conform to material standards and be galvanized to meet specified requirements.

IS 3989 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3989 :1984 CENTRIFUGALLY CAST (SPUN) IRON SPIGOT AND SOCKET SOIL WASTE AND VENTILATING PIPES FITTINGS AND ACCESSORIES (Second Revision)
IS 3989 : 1984.scope: This standard covers centrifugally cast (spun) iron spigot and socket soil, waste and ventilating pipes and fittings. These pipes and fittings are suitable for use above ground only. They must be capable of being cut with standard installation tools and meet specific hardness, soundness, and hydrostatic test requirements.

IS 5382 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5382 : 1985 RUBBER SEALING RINGS FOR GAS MAINS, WATER MAINS AND SEWERS (First Revision)
IS 5382 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements for vulcanized solid rubber sealing rings used in water supply and drainage systems, including gas pipes, sewers, and rainwater pipes. It covers joint rings for various piping materials such as iron, steel, concrete, and plastics, and includes joint design requirements.

IS 5531 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5531 : 1988 CAST IRON SPECIALS FOR ASBESTOS CEMENT PRESSURE PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE (First Revision)
IS 5531 : 1988.scope: This standard specifies requirements for cast iron specials designed for use with asbestos cement pressure pipes in water, gas, and sewage applications. These specials include detachable joints, reducers, crosses, spigots, tees, and bends, which are intended to connect asbestos cement pipes.

IS 6163 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6163 : 1978 CENTRIFUGALLY CAST (SPUN) IRON LOW PRESSURE PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE (First Revision)
IS 6163 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for centrifugally cast (spun) iron low pressure pipes, known as LP pipes, for conveying water, gas, and sewage. These pipes are manufactured in metal or sand moulds and must have spigots and sockets as specified, with rubber joints requiring maintained inner profile for safety and interchangeability.

IS 6418 : 1971.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6418 : 1971 CAST IRON AND MALLEABLE CAST IRON FLANGES FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES
IS 6418 : 1971.scope: This standard covers grey cast iron and malleable cast iron flanges for general engineering purposes, applicable for temperatures from 0 to 300°C and pressures for oil, water, steam, compressed air, and other non-corrosive fluids.

IS 7181 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF IS 7181 : 1986 HORIZONTALLY CAST IRON DOUBLE FLANGED PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE (First Revision)
IS 7181 : 1986.scope: This standard covers requirements for double flanged cast iron pipes of Class B up to dn 750, manufactured by horizontal castings in sand moulds, for use in water, gas, and sewage main lines.

IS 8329 : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8329 : 2000 CENTRIFUGALLY CAST (SPUN) DUCTILE IRON PRESSURE PIPES FOR WATER, GAS AND SEWAGE
IS 8329 : 2000.scope: Specifies the requirements and associated test methods applicable to ductile iron pipes manufactured in metal lined or unlined or sand moulds and their joints for the construction of pipe lines to convey water, sewage or gas, operated with or without pressure, and suitable for fluid temperatures between 0°C and 50°C, excluding frost.

IS 8794 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8794 : 1988 CAST IRON DETACHABLE JOINTS FOR USE WITH ASBESTOS CEMENT PRESSURE PIPES (First Revision)
IS 8794 : 1988.scope: This standard specifies requirements for cast iron detachable joints designed for use with asbestos cement pressure pipes conforming to IS 1592:1989. The joints must meet specified mechanical properties and dimensional tolerances to ensure compatibility and performance in pressure pipe systems.
