IS 2202 (PART 2) : 1983.scope: This standard covers requirements for material, grade, types, sizes, construction, finishes, and tests of wooden flush door shutters with solid core type, including particle board and hardboard face panels, both veneered and unveneered.

IS 4021 : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4021 : 1995 TIMBER DOOR, WINDOW AND VENTILATOR FRAMES (Third Revision)
IS 4021 : 1995.scope: This standard covers requirements for timber door, window and ventilator frames used in residential and institutional buildings. It specifies material, construction, workmanship, and sizes for these frames. It does not cover frames for commercial, industrial, or special buildings like workshops and garages.

IS 4962 : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4962 : 1968 WOODEN SIDE SLIDING DOORS
IS 4962 : 1968.scope: This standard covers requirements for materials, fabrication, assembly, and finish of wooden side sliding doors of the straight sliding type. It classifies doors by sliding mode into frame units and specifies materials for components like tracks, rollers, guides, brackets, and shutters. It also defines operational capabilities and finish specifications for these doors.

IS 6198 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6198 : 1992 LEDGED, BRACED AND BATTENED TIMBER DOOR SHUTTERS (Second Revision)
IS 6198 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the material, sizes, construction, workmanship, and finish of ledged, braced, and battened timber door shutters. It covers the classification and designation of shutters based on width, type, and height, ensuring durability and proper fit within door openings. The standard also outlines dimensional tolerances and testing procedures for quality assurance.

IS 15380 : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF IS 15380 : 2003 MOULDED RAISED HIGH DENSITY FIBRE (HDF) PANEL DOORS
IS 15380 : 2003.scope: This standard lays down requirements regarding types, sizes, material, construction, workmanship and finish and tests for high density fibre (HDF) panel doors. Door shutters shall be of following two types: Heavy Duty with void area less than 35 percent, and Light Duty with void area not exceeding 65 percent. The nominal thickness of the shutters shall be 30 mm, 35 mm and 40 mm.

IS 1038 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1038 : 1983 STEEL DOOR, WINDOWS AND VENTILATORS (Fifth Revision)
IS 1038 : 1983.scope: This standard covers the requirements for material, fabrication, and finish of steel doors, windows, ventilators, and fixed-lights manufactured from rolled steel sections to specified sizes and designs. It does not cover steel doors, windows, ventilators, and fixed-lights for use in industrial buildings.

IS 1361 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1361 : 1978 STEEL WINDOWS FOR INDUSTRIAL BUILDINGS (First Revision)
IS 1361 : 1978.scope: This standard deals with steel windows suitable for use in industrial buildings, based on a 10 cm module. It covers the design, sizing, and manufacturing tolerances for sashes, ventilators, and coupling members, ensuring they fit modular openings with specified clearances.

IS 1948 : 1961.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1948 : 1961 ALUMINIUM DOORS, WINDOWS AND VENTILATORS
IS 1948 : 1961.scope: This standard covers requirements for aluminium doors, windows, and ventilators made from extruded aluminium alloy sections of standard sizes and designs, complete with fittings, intended for industrial buildings. It specifies material, fabrication, dimensions, and designations for these products.

IS 1949 : 1961.title: SUMMARY OF ALUMINIUM WINDOWS FOR INDUSTRIAL BUILDINGS
IS 1949 : 1961.scope: This standard deals with aluminium windows suitable for use in industrial buildings, designed to suit openings based on a module of 10 cm. It covers the designation, sizes, materials, and fitting of aluminium windows, including fixed, centre-hung, and bottom-hung sashes, as well as ventilators and composite windows.

IS 4351 : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF STEEL DOOR FRAMES (Second Revision)
IS 4351 : 2003.scope: This standard covers requirements for the material, dimensions, and construction of steel door frames for internal and external use, specifying materials conforming to relevant Indian standards and detailing components such as base ties, hinges, lock-strike plates, and shock absorbers.

IS 6248 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6248 : 1979 METAL ROLLING SHUTTERS AND ROLLING GRILLS (First Revision)
IS 6248 : 1979.scope: This standard covers requirements for materials, fabrication, and finish of metal rolling shutters and rolling grills, with a focus on their design, operation, and safety features. It applies to both rolling shutters and rolling grills, noting that rolling shutters are more commonly referenced, but rolling grills are also covered. The standard specifies dimensions, types, and safety devices for these products.

IS 7452 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF HOT ROLLED STEEL SECTIONS FOR DOORS, WINDOWS AND VENTILATORS (Second Revision)
IS 7452 : 1990.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hot rolled steel sections used in doors, windows, and ventilators, covering materials, dimensions, mass, surface finish, and packing. It outlines tolerances for thickness, radii of curvature, and mass, ensuring suitability for manufacturing and handling.

IS 10451 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 10451 : 1983 STEEL SLIDING SHUTTERS (TOP HUNG TYPE)
IS 10451 : 1983.scope: This standard specifies requirements for materials, type, shape, fabrication, assembly, and finish of top hung steel sliding shutters. It ensures weather protection by requiring shutter sizes to be larger than the actual opening. The standard covers fabrication details including track and runner specifications for smooth operation.

IS 10521 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF COLLAPSIBLE GATES
IS 10521 : 1983.scope: This standard covers requirements for materials, fabrication, and finish of collapsible gates. It specifies types, sizes, and materials for different gate configurations such as those fixed under lintels, inside openings, or with swinging arrangements. The standard also addresses fabrication details and finish requirements for collapsible gate components.

IS 14856 : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14856 : 2000 GLASS FIBRE REINFORCED PLASTIC (GRP) PANEL TYPE DOOR SHUTTERS FOR INTERNAL USE
IS 14856 : 2000.scope: This standard specifies requirements for glass fibre reinforced plastic (GRP) panel type door shutters for internal use in residential and industrial buildings, covering material, construction, workmanship, finish, and performance.

IS 432 (PART 1) : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 432 (PART 1) : 1982 MILD STEEL AND MEDIUM TENSILE STEEL BARS AND HARD-DRAWN STEEL WIRE FOR CONCRETE REINFORCEMENT
IS 432 (PART 1) : 1982.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for mild steel and medium tensile steel bars used as reinforcement in concrete. It covers plain bars and square sections, including their nominal sizes, tolerances, and mechanical properties. The standard also addresses the suitability of Grade II bars for structures in earthquake zones or under dynamic loading.

IS 1608 : 1995.title: Plain Hard-Drawn Steel Wire for Prestressed Concrete (Part I Cold Drawn Stress-Relieved Wire)
IS 1608 : 1995.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the manufacture, supply, and testing of plain, cold-drawn, stress-relieved steel wire used in prestressed concrete. It covers nominal sizes, chemical composition, physical properties, and tolerances for wire diameters of 2.50, 3.00, 4.00, 5.00, 7.00, and 8.00 mm.

IS 1566 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1566 : 1982 HARD-DRAWN STEEL WIRE FABRIC FOR CONCRETE REINFORCEMENT (Second Revision)
IS 1566 : 1982.scope: This standard covers requirements for hard-drawn steel wire fabric, electrically welded for use as concrete reinforcement. It specifies types (oblong and square mesh), material (hard-drawn steel wire suitable for welding), and dimensions for sheets or rolls. The fabric is designed for concrete reinforcement applications.

IS 1785 (PART 2) : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1785 (PART 2) : 1983 PLAIN HARD-DRAWN STEEL WIRE FOR PRESTRESSED CONCRETE. PART 2 - AS DRAWN WIRE (First Revision)
IS 1785 (PART 2) : 1983.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the manufacture, supply, and testing of plain 'as-drawn' steel wire used in prestressed concrete pipes and similar purposes. It covers chemical composition, nominal sizes, tolerances, tensile strength, proof stress, ductility, and coiling characteristics of the wire.

IS 1786 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1786 : 1985 PLAIN HIGH STRENGTH DEFORMED STEEL BARS AND WIRES FOR CONCRETE REINFORCEMENT (Third Revision)
IS 1786 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for deformed steel bars and wires used as reinforcement in concrete. It covers three strength grades: Fe 415, Fe 500, and Fe 550, defining their chemical composition, nominal sizes, cross-sectional areas, masses, and mechanical properties.

IS 2090 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2090 : 1983 HIGH TENSILE STEEL BARS USED IN PRESTRESSED CONCRETE (First Revision)
IS 2090 : 1983.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for high tensile steel bars used in prestressed concrete. It covers chemical composition, nominal sizes, tolerances, and physical properties such as tensile strength and elongation at rupture. The standard ensures quality and consistency for structural applications in prestressed concrete construction.

IS 6003 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6003 : 1983 INDENTED WIRE FOR PRESTRESSED CONCRETE (First Revision)
IS 6003 : 1983.scope: This standard specifies requirements for the manufacture, supply, and testing of intended hard-drawn and stress-relieved wire intended for use in prestressed concrete. It covers chemical composition, nominal sizes, geometrical characteristics, tolerances, and physical requirements such as tensile strength, proof stress, ductility, elongation after fracture, and relaxation stress.

IS 6006 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6006 : 1983 UNCOATED STRESS RELIEVED STRAND FOR PRESTRESSED CONCRETE (First Revision)
IS 6006 : 1983.scope: This standard covers requirements for the manufacture, supply, and testing of uncoated, stress-relieved, high-tensile steel strands for use in prestressed concrete. It specifies the types of strands, including two-wire, three-wire, and seven-wire strands (Class 1 and Class 2), along with their dimensions, tolerances, and physical properties.

IS 7887 : 1992.title: SUMMARY OF IS 7887 : 1992 MILD STEEL WIRE ROD FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES (First Revision)
IS 7887 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for hot-rolled mild steel wire rods for general engineering purposes, covering chemical composition, product analysis, size, tolerances, and freedom from defects. The material is supplied in coils or straightened cut lengths, with grades ranging from 1 to 10M.

IS 13620 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13620 : 1993 FUSION BONDED EPOXY COATED REINFORCING BARS (First Revision)
IS 13620 : 1993.scope: This standard covers deformed steel reinforcing bars with protective epoxy coating applied by electrostatic spray method. It specifies requirements for coating materials, surface preparation, application, and finished bar characteristics to ensure durability and compatibility with concrete.

IS 14268 : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14268 : 1995 UNCOATED STRESS RELIEVED LOW RELAXATION SEVEN-PLY STRAND FOR PRESTRESSED CONCRETE
IS 14268 : 1995.scope: This standard covers the requirements for the manufacture, supply, and testing of un-coated, stress relieved 'low relaxation' seven-ply steel strand for prestressed concrete. It specifies physical properties, mechanical properties, and relaxation characteristics for Class I and Class II strands.

IS 1977 : 1996.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1977 : 1996 LOW TENSILE STRUCTURAL STEEL (Third Revision)
IS 1977 : 1996.scope: This standard covers low carbon steel plates, sections, flats, and bars for general structural purposes, with tensile strengths ranging from 290 to 470 MPa. The steels are suitable for bolted and riveted structures and general engineering applications.

IS 2062 : 1999.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2062 : 1999 STEEL FOR GENERAL STRUCTURAL PURPOSES (Fifth Revision)
IS 2062 : 1999.scope: This standard specifies requirements for steel plates, strips, sections, flats, and bars for use in welded, bolted, and riveted structures, and general engineering purposes. It covers grades A, B, and C, with specific mechanical properties and welding recommendations.

IS 8500 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8500 : 1991 STRUCTURAL STEEL- MICRO ALLOYED (MEDIUM AND HIGH STRENGTH QUALITIES)
IS 8500 : 1991.scope: This standard covers the requirements of microalloyed steels of different strength levels for use in structural work. These steels may also be suitable for other applications. The finished material must be free from surface defects and meet specified mechanical properties.

IS 11587 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF STRUCTURAL WEATHER RESISTANCE STEELS
IS 11587 : 1986.scope: This standard specifies requirements for high strength low alloy weather resistant structural steels in the form of plates, strips, sections and bars for welded, riveted or bolted construction requiring atmospheric corrosion resistance.

IS 277 : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF IS 277 : 2003 GALVANIZED STEEL SHEETS (PLAIN AND CORRUGATED) (Sixth Revision)
IS 277 : 2003.scope: This standard covers the requirements for plain and corrugated galvanized steel sheets and strips, including classification, zinc coating mass, bend test, freedom from defects, and dimensional tolerances. It specifies grades for different thicknesses and provides guidelines for manufacturing and quality control.

IS 412 : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 412 : 1975 EXPANDED METAL STEEL SHEETS FOR GENERAL PURPOSES (Second Revision)
IS 412 : 1975.scope: This standard specifies requirements for expanded metal steel sheets used for general purposes. It covers the size of mesh (shortway and longway), dimensions of sheets, and mechanical properties. The standard is intended for the production and use of expanded metal steel sheets in various construction and industrial applications.

IS 513 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 513 : 1994 COLD ROLLED LOW CARBON STEEL SHEETS AND STRIPS (Fourth Revision)
IS 513 : 1994.scope: This standard covers cold rolled low carbon steel sheets and strips up to 4 mm thick, specifically for bending and drawing purposes, in both coil and cut length forms. It specifies requirements for grades O, D, DD, and EDD, including chemical composition, mechanical properties, and dimensional tolerances.

IS 1079 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1079 : 1994 HOT ROLLED CARBON STEEL SHEETS AND STRIPS (Fifth Revision)
IS 1079 : 1994.scope: This standard covers requirements for hot rolled carbon steel sheets and strips intended for cold forming, drawing, and general engineering purposes. It specifies grades (O, D, DD, EDD) with different quality levels for fabrication, drawing, and severe forming applications. The standard includes mechanical properties, bend tests, cupping tests, and dimensional tolerances.

IS 3502 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3502 : 1994 STEEL CHEQUERED PLATES (Second Revision)
IS 3502 : 1994.scope: This standard covers requirements for steel chequered plates with raised figures at regular intervals on one surface, intended for general structural purposes. These plates are supplied with sheared edges and must conform to specified material, dimensional, and mechanical properties.

IS 7226 : 1974.title: SUMMARY OF IS 7226 : 1974 COLD-ROLLED MEDIUM, HIGH CARBON AND LOW ALLOY STEEL STRIP FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES
IS 7226 : 1974.scope: This standard covers requirements for cold-rolled medium, high carbon, and low alloy steel strips with thickness up to 3 mm and width up to 330 mm, intended for general engineering purposes.

IS 12313 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12313 : 1988 HOT-DIP TERNE COATED CARBON STEEL SHEETS
IS 12313 : 1988.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hot-dip terne coated carbon steel sheets used in the automobile industry and other similar applications. The sheets have a thickness between 0.3 to 2.0 mm, with thicknesses outside this range agreed upon by manufacturer and purchaser. The coating mass and quality are designated using a system combining coating designation, quality, and coating mass.

IS 1148 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1148 : 1982 HOT ROLLED STEEL RIVETS BARS (UPTO 40 MM DIAMETER) FOR STRUCTURAL PURPOSES (Third Revision)
IS 1148 : 1982.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hot-rolled steel rivet bars up to 40 mm diameter, used for manufacturing hot forged rivets for structural purposes. It covers chemical composition, product analysis, dimensional tolerances, and mechanical testing requirements. The material must be free from surface and internal flaws and meet specified tensile, yield, and shear strength criteria.

IS 1149 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF HIGH TENSILE STEEL RIVETS BARS FOR STRUCTURAL PURPOSES (Third Revision)
IS 1149 : 1982.scope: This standard specifies requirements for high tensile steel rivet bars used for structural purposes, covering chemical composition, dimensional tolerances, and mechanical tests. It applies to bars with diameters up to 40 mm, ensuring quality for structural applications through defined limits for carbon, sulphur, phosphorus, and silicon content.

IS 1161 : 1998.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1161 : 1998 STEEL TUBES FOR STRUCTURAL PURPOSES (Fourth Revision)
IS 1161 : 1998.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hot finished welded (HFW), hot finished seamless (HFS), and electric resistance welded (ERW) or high frequency induction welded (HRIW) plain carbon steel tubes for structural purposes. The tubes are classified by nominal bore and wall thickness, and further graded by yield stress (YSt 210, YSt 240, YSt 310).

IS 4923 : 1997.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4923 : 1997 HOLLOW STEEL SECTIONS FOR STRUCTURAL USE (Second Revision)
IS 4923 : 1997.scope: This standard covers requirements for hot and cold formed square and rectangular hollow steel sections for structural use. It specifies designation, dimensions, weights, and tolerances for these sections, including both cold formed (CF) and hot formed (HF) types.

IS 733 : 1983.title: WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ALUMINIUM ALLOY BARS, RODS AND SECTIONS FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES
IS 733 : 1983.scope: This standard covers wrought aluminium and aluminium alloy bars, rods, and sections for general engineering purposes. The material must be sound and free from harmful defects. Dimensions and tolerances are as per IS 3965:1981.

IS 2677 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF DIMENSIONS FOR WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ALLOYS PLATES AND HOT-ROLLED SHEETS
IS 2677 : 1979.scope: This standard lays down the dimensions and tolerances for wrought aluminium and aluminium alloys plates and hot-rolled sheets. It specifies dimensions for various thicknesses, widths, and lengths, along with tolerances for thickness, length, and width of the plates and sheets.

IS 2676 : 1981.title: SUMMARY OF DIMENSIONS FOR WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ALUMINIUM ALLOYS, SHEET AND STRIP (Second Revision)
IS 2676 : 1981.scope: This standard lays down dimensions and tolerances for wrought aluminium alloys, sheet and strip. It specifies dimensions for various lengths and widths, along with thicknesses and squareness tolerances for sheets and strips. Detailed specifications refer to the first revision of IS 2676:1981.

IS 738 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 738 : 1994 WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ITS ALLOYS-DRAWN TUBES FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES (Third Revision)
IS 738 : 1994.scope: This standard covers the requirements for wrought aluminium and aluminium alloy drawn tubes of round cross-section for general engineering purposes. The tubes must be free from harmful defects and conform to specified dimensions and tolerances. It provides designation and typical uses for various alloys.

IS 739 : 1992.title: Wrought Aluminium and Aluminium Alloys-Wire for General Engineering Purposes (Third Revision)
IS 739 : 1992.scope: This standard specifies requirements for wrought aluminium and aluminium alloy wire intended for general engineering purposes. The wire must be free from harmful defects and meet specified dimensions and tolerances. It covers various alloy designations with typical uses in industries such as construction, marine, food processing, and manufacturing.

IS 740 : 1977.title: SUMMARY OF ALUMINIUM ALLOY RIVET STOCK FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES
IS 740 : 1977.scope: This standard covers wrought aluminium and aluminium alloy rivet stock for general engineering purposes, requiring the material to be sound and free from harmful defects. It specifies dimensions, tolerances, and typical uses for various alloys.

IS 1254 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1254 : 1991 CORRUGATED ALUMINIUM SHEET (First Revision)
IS 1254 : 1991.scope: This standard covers corrugated aluminium sheets intended for general purpose, industrial, and building uses. It specifies material, profile, dimensions, and finish requirements for these sheets. The sheets must be clean and free from harmful defects.

IS 1285 : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1285 : 2002 WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ALUMINIUM ALLOY EXTRUDED ROUND TUBE AND HOLLOW SECTIONS FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES (Third Revision)
IS 1285 : 2002.scope: This standard covers requirements for extruded round tubes and hollow sections made from wrought aluminum and aluminum alloys for general engineering purposes. These materials must be sound and free from harmful defects. Dimensions and tolerances are specified according to IS 2673:2002 and IS 6477:1983.

IS 2525 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF DIMENSION FOR WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ALUMINIUM ALLOYS, WIRE (First Revision)
IS 2525 : 1982.scope: This standard lays down dimensions and tolerances for wrought aluminium alloys in the form of wire. It specifies diameters of round wires and width/width across flats of shaped wires. The standard covers various wire types including round, hexagonal, octagonal, square, and rectangular wires with defined dimensional tolerances.

IS 2678 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF DIMENSIONS AND TOLERANCE FOR WROUGHT ALUMINIUM AND ALUMINIUM ALLOYS DRAWN ROUND TUBES (Second Revision)
IS 2678 : 1987.scope: This standard lays down the dimensions and tolerances for wrought aluminium and aluminium alloy drawn round tubes with parallel bore. It provides specifications for nominal outside diameters, wall thicknesses, and tolerances on wall thickness, outside or inside diameters, and straightness.

IS 3908 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF ALUMINIUM EQUAL LEG ANGLES (First Revision)
IS 3908 : 1986.scope: This standard covers the material, dimensions, and sectional properties of aluminium equal leg angles for structural use and other applications. Aluminium equal leg angles are designated as ALE followed by lengths of legs and thickness of the section in mm. The standard specifies dimensions and materials for various sizes of these angles.

IS 3909 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3909 : 1986 ALUMINIUM UNEQUAL LEG ANGLES (First Revision)
IS 3909 : 1986.scope: Covers the material, dimensions and sectional properties of aluminium unequal leg angles for structural use and other applications. Designation follows ALU followed by lengths of longer and shorter legs and thickness of the section in mm. For example, Alu 80 × 60 × 6.

IS 3921 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF ALUMINIUM CHANNELS (First Revision)
IS 3921 : 1985.scope: This standard covers the material, dimensions, and sectional properties of aluminium channels for structural use and other applications. Aluminium channels are designated as ALC followed by the depth and flange width in mm, and the mass of the section in kg/m.

IS 5384 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF ALUMINIUM I-BEAM (First Revision)
IS 5384 : 1985.scope: Covers the material, dimensions and sectional properties of aluminium I-beam sections for structural use and other applications. Aluminium I-beam sections shall be designated as ALB followed by the width of flange in mm and mass in kilograms per metre. The standard specifies materials and alloys used for manufacturing these sections.

IS 6445 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF ALUMINIUM TEE - SECTIONS (First Revision)
IS 6445 : 1985.scope: Covers the material, dimensions and sectional properties of aluminium tee-sections for structural use and other applications. Aluminium tee-sections shall be designated as ALT followed by the depth of section in mm and mass in kilograms per metre of the section. The standard covers aluminium sections manufactured from specific alloys in appropriate temper for general engineering purposes.

IS 808 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 808 : 1989 DIMENSIONS FOR HOT ROLLED STEELBEAM, COLUMN, CHANNEL AND ANGLE SECTIONS (Third Revision)
IS 808 : 1989.scope: Covers the nominal dimensions and sectional properties of hot rolled sloping flange beam, column sections, sloping and parallel flange channel sections and equal and unequal leg angle sections.

IS 811 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF IS 811 : 1987 COLD FORMED LIGHT GAUGE STRUCTURAL STEEL SECTIONS (Second Revision)
IS 811 : 1987.scope: This standard lays down dimensions, mass, and sectional properties for cold formed light gauge open wall steel sections for structural and other general applications, with a minimum thickness of 1.25mm. Cold formed sections are designated by figures denoting depth × width × thickness (mm).

IS 1173 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1173 : 1978 HOT ROLLED AND SLIT STEEL TEE BARS (Second Revision)
IS 1173 : 1978.scope: This standard lays down nominal dimensions, weight, and basic geometrical properties for hot rolled and slit steel tee bars. It classifies these bars into different weight categories and provides dimensions and moments of inertia for various sizes. The standard is intended for use in construction and structural applications requiring tee bars.

IS 1730 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1730 : 1989 STEEL PLATES SHEETS STRIPS AND FLATS, FOR STRUCTURAL AND GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES (Second Revision)
IS 1730 : 1989.scope: Specifies nominal dimensions, mass and surface area for hot-rolled steel plates, sheets, strips and flats for structural and general engineering purposes.

IS 1732 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1732 : 1989 STEEL BARS, ROUND AND SQUARE FOR STRUCTURAL AND GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES-DIMENSIONS (Second Revision)
IS 1732 : 1989.scope: Specifies dimensions, sectional areas and mass of hot-rolled round and square steel bars for structural and general engineering purposes. This standard does not cover bars for rivets and threaded components.

IS 1863 : 1979.title: SUMMARY OF ROLLED STEEL BULB FLATS (First Revision)
IS 1863 : 1979.scope: Specifies dimensions, sectional properties and dimensional tolerances of hot-rolled steel bulb flats. The bulb flats may be manufactured from steel conforming to IS 2062:1992, IS 3039:1988 or IS 8500:1991 as appropriate. Designation is by width (b) and thickness (t).

IS 2314 : 1986.title: SUMMARY OF STEEL SHEET PILING SECTIONS (First Revision)
IS 2314 : 1986.scope: This standard stipulates dimensions and dimensional tolerances for Z-type, U-type, and flat-type hot-rolled steel sheet piling sections, including their sectional properties calculated from nominal dimensions. It specifies that the material must conform to IS 2062:1992* or IS 8500:1991*, with copper content between 0.20 and 0.35 percent for copper-bearing steel.

IS 3443 : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3443 : 1980 CRANE RAIL SECTION (First Revision)
IS 3443 : 1980.scope: This standard lays down the dimensions, shape, and other requirements for crane rail sections. It specifies the designation, tensile properties, hardness, and freedom from defects for these sections. The standard covers steel rails with specific mechanical and dimensional tolerances.

IS 3954 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF HOT ROLLED CHANNEL SECTIONS FOR GENERAL ENGINEERING PURPOSES – DIMENSIONS (First Revision)
IS 3954 : 1991.scope: Lays down the nominal dimensions, mass and sectional properties of hot-rolled steel channel sections for general engineering purposes. Hot rolled steel channel sections conforming to this standard shall be designated by the letters CHG followed by a figure denoting the depth of the channel in mm.

IS 3964 : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3964 : 1980 LIGHT RAILS (First Revision)
IS 3964 : 1980.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for light rails, including designation, tensile properties, dimensions, sectional properties, tolerances, and freedom from defects. It covers steel rails with minimum tensile strength of 710 MPa and minimum elongation of 14 percent. The designation uses letters ISLR followed by a figure denoting weight per meter.

IS 8081 : 1976.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8081 : 1976 SLOTTED SECTIONS
IS 8081 : 1976.scope: This standard covers the requirements for materials, workmanship, finish, strength tests, and general design provisions for slotted sections used in metal shelving. It does not apply to steel sections used for adjustable metal shelving cabinets or racks. Steel and aluminum sections must conform to specified grades and be free from burrs and surface imperfections.

IS 12778 : 2004.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12778 : 2004 HOT ROLLED PARALLEL FLANGE STEEL SECTION FOR BEAMS, COLUMNS AND BEARING PILES DIMENSIONS AND SECTION PROPERTIES (First Revision)
IS 12778 : 2004.scope: Covers the nominal dimensions, mass and sectional properties of hot rolled parallel flange beams, columns and bearing piles. The standard classifies these sections into narrow and wide parallel flange beams and bearing piles, using abbreviated reference symbols for designation.

IS 814 : 2004.title: SUMMARY OF IS 814 : 2004 COVERED ELECTRODES FOR MANUAL METAL ARC WELDING OF CARBON AND CARBON MANGANESE STEEL (Sixth Revision)
IS 814 : 2004.scope: This standard covers the requirements for covered carbon and carbon manganese steel electrodes for manual metal arc welding, including hydrogen controlled electrodes for mild and medium tensile steels. It specifies coding systems for strength, elongation, impact properties, and welding conditions, and includes provisions for repair welding and radiographic quality electrodes.

IS 1278 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF FILLER RODS FOR GAS WELDING (Second Revision)
IS 1278 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies requirements for filler rods used in gas welding, covering ferrous and non-ferrous materials such as structural steels, austenitic stainless steels, cast irons, copper and copper alloys, nickel and nickel alloys, aluminum and aluminum alloys, and magnesium and magnesium alloys. It defines dimensions, tolerances, and surface requirements for these rods.

IS 1395 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1395 : 1982 LOW AND MEDIUM ALLOY STEEL COVERED ELECTRODES FOR MANUAL METAL ARC WELDING (Third Revision)
IS 1395 : 1982.scope: Covers the requirements for low and medium alloy steel covered electrodes for manual metal arc welding. These electrodes are classified by tensile strength, flux covering type, chemical composition, and welding position. They are used for manual metal arc welding applications.

IS 4972 : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF RESISTANCE SPOT - WELDING ELECTRODES
IS 4972 : 1968.scope: This standard covers code numbers, dimensional requirements, and physical and mechanical properties for a series of spot-welding electrodes, cap electrodes, and shanks, mainly intended for resistance spot welding of ferrous and non-ferrous metals.

IS 5511 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5511 : 1991 COVERED ELECTRODES FOR MANUAL METAL ARC WELDING OF CAST IRON (First Revision)
IS 5511 : 1991.scope: This standard specifies a system of classification and coding for covered electrodes used in manual metal arc welding of cast iron. It covers the requirements for electrode composition, coating type, welding position, current type, and physical dimensions. The standard aims to ensure consistent quality and performance for welding cast iron applications.

IS 5897 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5897 : 1985 ALUMINIUM AND ALUMINIUM ALLOY WELDING RODS AND WIRES AND MAGNESIUM ALLOY WELDING RODS (First Revision)
IS 5897 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements for bare solid filler rods and wires used in welding aluminum, aluminum alloys, and magnesium alloys via inert gas tungsten arc (TIG) or gas metal arc (MIG) welding processes. It covers dimensions, tolerances, chemical composition, and suitability for specific welding gases, but does not specify mechanical properties or weld deposit characteristics.

IS 5898 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5898 : 1970 COPPER AND COPPER ALLOY BARE SOLID WELDING RODS AND ELECTRODES
IS 5898 : 1970.scope: This standard specifies requirements for bare solid filler rods and wires for welding copper and copper alloys using inert-gas tungsten arc welding (TIG) or gas metal arc welding (MIG). It covers the chemical composition and dimensions of these rods and wires, but does not specify mechanical properties of the weld deposit.

IS 6560 : 1996.title: SUMMARY OF MOLYBDENUM AND CHROMIUM-MOLYBDENUM LOW ALLOY STEEL WELDING RODS AND BARE ELECTRODES FOR GAS SHIELDED ARC WELDING
IS 6560 : 1996.scope: This standard covers molybdenum and chromium-molybdenum low alloy steel rods and wires for use in inert-gas tungsten arc welding (TIG), gas metal arc welding (MIG), or CO2 welding processes. It specifies the mechanical properties of the weld deposits and the dimensions and tolerances of the filler rods and wires.

IS 7280 : 1974.title: SUMMARY OF BARE WIRE ELECTRODES FOR SUBMERGED ARC WELDING OF STRUCTURAL STEELS
IS 7280 : 1974.scope: This standard specifies requirements for solid filler wires used in submerged arc welding of structural steels, including dimensions, tolerances, and chemical composition classifications. It serves as a guide for manufacturers and selectors of bare wire electrodes for submerged arc welding of structural steels.

IS 8363 : 1976.title: Summary of IS 8363 : 1976 Bare Wire Electrodes for Electroslag Welding of Steels
IS 8363 : 1976.scope: This standard provides guidelines for manufacturers and users regarding the selection of solid bare wire electrodes for electroslag welding of carbon and low alloy steels. It specifies requirements for wire dimensions, tolerances, surface finish, and chemical composition to ensure uniform feeding in automatic or semi-automatic welding equipment.

IS 207 : 1964.title: SUMMARY OF IS 207 : 1964 GATE AND SHUTTER HOOKS AND EYES (Revised)
IS 207 : 1964.scope: This standard covers requirements for gate and shutter hooks and eyes commonly used on doors and windows to keep them in position when kept open. It specifies types, dimensions, tolerances, and finish for mild steel and cast brass components.

IS 723 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 723 : 1972 STEEL COUNTER SUNK HEAD WIRE NAILS (Second Revision)
IS 723 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for steel countersunk head wire nails, including dimensions, tolerances, and test procedures. It covers the designations, finish, and mechanical properties of these nails used in construction. The standard ensures uniformity in the production and quality of steel wire nails for structural and fastening applications.

IS 724 : 1964.title: SUMMARY OF IS 724 : 1964 MILD STEEL AND BRASS CUP, RULER AND SQUARE HOOKS AND SCREW EYES (Revised)
IS 724 : 1964.scope: This standard specifies requirements for mild steel and brass cup, ruler, and square hooks and screw eyes. It covers various types including shouldered and plain hooks, and screw eyes, with designations based on type, length, and other dimensions. The standard ensures uniformity in the production and use of these fasteners for structural and mechanical applications.

IS 725 : 1961.title: SUMMARY OF IS 725 : 1961 COPPER WIRE NAILS (Revised)
IS 725 : 1961.scope: This standard covers copper wire nails of various types including rose-head, countersunk-head, and cut-lath nails. It specifies dimensions, head sizes, and finish requirements for these nails, with particular attention to shank diameter, point types, and coating for tinned nails.

IS 730 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 730 : 1978 HOOK BOLTS FOR CORRUGATED SHEET ROOFING (Second Revision)
IS 730 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hook bolts used in corrugated sheet roofing. It covers types (J, L, U), dimensions, grades, mechanical properties, and designation methods for these fasteners. The standard is intended for use in construction applications involving corrugated metal roofing systems.

IS 1120 : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1120 : 1975 COACH SCREW (First Revision)
IS 1120 : 1975.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for hexagon head coach screws, which are used in wood applications. It covers dimensions, tolerances, and designation rules for screws made of steel. The standard also references additional details for unaddressed requirements.

IS 1363 (Part 1) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF ISO 4016 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS AND NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADE C PART 1: HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS (SIZE RANGE M5 TO M64) (Fourth Revision)
IS 1363 (Part 1) : 2002.scope: This standard gives specifications for hexagon head bolts, screws, and nuts of product grade C with threads from M5 up to and including M64. It covers preferred and non-preferred thread sizes and references international standards for material, mechanical properties, tolerances, and finish requirements.

IS 1363 (PART 2) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1363 (PART 2) : 2002 ISO 4018 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS & NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADE C PART 2 : HEXAGON HEAD SCREWS (SIZE RANGE M5 TO M64) (Fourth Revision)
IS 1363 (PART 2) : 2002.scope: This standard provides specifications for hexagon head screws with threads from M5 up to and including M64 of product grade C. It covers dimensions and specifications for both preferred and non-preferred thread sizes, and references international standards for material, mechanical properties, and tolerances. The standard also includes guidelines for finish, acceptability, and designation of screws.

IS 1363 (PART 3) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF ISO 4034 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS & NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADE C PART 3 : HEXAGON NUTS (SIZE RANGE M5 TO M64)
IS 1363 (PART 3) : 2002.scope: This standard gives specifications for hexagon nuts with thread diameters from M5 to M64 inclusive and product grade C. It covers dimensions, material, mechanical properties, tolerances, and finish requirements for hexagon nuts. The standard also includes designation examples and references to international standards.

IS 1364 (Part 1) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF ISO 4014 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS AND NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADES A AND B PART 1: HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS (SIZE RANGE M1.6 TO M64) (Third Revision)
IS 1364 (Part 1) : 2002.scope: This standard gives specifications for hexagon head bolts with threads from M1.6 to M64, product grade A for threads M1.6 to M24 and nominal lengths up to and including 10 d or 150 mm, and product grade B for threads over M24 or nominal lengths over 10 d or 150 mm, whichever is shorter.

IS 1364 (PART 2) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF ISO 4017 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS & NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADE A & B PART 2: HEXAGON HEAD SCREWS (SIZE RANGE 1.6 TO M64)
IS 1364 (PART 2) : 2002.scope: This standard provides specifications for hexagon head screws with threads from M1.6 to M64 and nominal lengths up to 10d or 150 mm, categorized by product grade A and B based on thread size and length. It covers both preferred and non-preferred thread sizes and includes dimensional, material, and finish requirements.

IS 1364 (Part 3) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF ISO 4032 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS AND NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADES A & B PART 3: HEXAGON NUTS (SIZE RANGE M1.6 TO M64)
IS 1364 (Part 3) : 2002.scope: This standard provides specifications for hexagon nuts with thread diameters from M1.6 to M64, categorized by product grade A for threads up to M16 and product grade B for threads greater than M16. It covers preferred and non-preferred thread sizes and includes dimensional and material requirements for steel, stainless steel, and non-ferrous metals.

IS 1364 (PART 4) : 2003.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1364 (PART 4) : 2003 ISO 4035 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS,SCREWS&NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADE A & B (SIZE RANGE M1.6 TO M64)
IS 1364 (PART 4) : 2003.scope: This standard gives specifications for chamfered hexagon thin nuts with thread diameters from M1.6 to M64, including product grade A for threads d ≤ M16 and product grade B for threads d > M16. It covers preferred and non-preferred thread sizes and references international standards for material, mechanical properties, tolerances, and finish requirements.

IS 1364 (Part 5) : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1364 (PART 5): 2002 ISO 4036 : 1999 HEXAGON HEAD BOLTS, SCREWS AND NUTS OF PRODUCT GRADES A AND B PART 5 HEXAGON THIN NUTS (UNCHAMFERED) (SIZE RANGE M1.6 TO M10) (Third Revision)
IS 1364 (Part 5) : 2002.scope: Gives specifications for hexagon thin nuts with metric dimensions and thread diameters from 1.6 mm up to and including 10 mm and product grade B.

IS 1365 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1365 : 1978 SLOTTED COUNTERSUNK HEAD SCREWS (Third Revision)
IS 1365 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for slotted countersunk head screws with diameters ranging from 1 to 20 mm. It covers mechanical properties, designation, and dimensions for threaded fasteners made of brass or aluminium alloy. The screws are designed for general mechanical fastening applications.

IS 1366 : 2002.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1366 : 2002 ISO 1207 - 1992 SLOTTED CHEESE HEAD SCREWS (Third Revision)
IS 1366 : 2002.scope: This standard covers requirements for slotted cheese head screws of product grade A with heads from M1.6 to M10. It specifies dimensions, material, mechanical properties, tolerances, and finish requirements for these screws.

IS 1929 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1929 : 1982 HOT FORGED STEEL RIVETS FOR HOT CLOSING (12 TO 36 MM DIAMETER)
IS 1929 : 1982.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hot forged solid mild steel and high tensile steel rivets (snap head, flat countersunk, and flat head) with diameters ranging from 12 to 36 mm, intended for general engineering purposes involving hot closing.

IS 2016 : 1967.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2016 : 1967 PLAIN WASHERS (First Revision)
IS 2016 : 1967.scope: This standard specifies requirements for plain washers, including machined and punched types, for general purpose bolts and screws. It covers various sizes, materials, and finishes, with specific dimensions and tolerances defined for different bolt/screw sizes.

IS 2155 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2155 : 1982 COLD FORGED SOLID STEEL RIVETS FOR HOT CLOSING (6 TO 16 mm DIAMETER) (First Revision)
IS 2155 : 1982.scope: This standard covers cold forged solid steel rivets with diameters ranging from 6 to 16 mm, intended for general engineering purposes and specifically for hot closing applications. It specifies dimensions, tolerances, and test requirements for these rivets to ensure structural integrity and performance in fastening applications.

IS 2585 : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2585 : 1968 BLACK SQUARE BOLTS AND NUTS (DIAMETER RANGE 6 TO 39 MM) AND BLACK SQUARE SCREWS (DIAMETER RANGE 6 TO 24 MM)
IS 2585 : 1968.scope: This standard covers black square bolts and nuts with diameters ranging from 6 to 39 mm, and black square screws with diameters ranging from 6 to 24 mm. It specifies requirements, designations, and mechanical properties for these fasteners, ensuring they conform to property class 4.6 for bolts and screws, and grade B for nuts. The standard provides preferred lengths and dimensional details for various sizes.

IS 2687 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2687 : 1991 CAP NUTS (Second Revision)
IS 2687 : 1991.scope: This standard specifies requirements for cap nuts of product Grade A in the size range M4 to M48 with coarse and fine pitch threads. It covers technical supply conditions, dimensions, designation, and mechanical properties for cap nuts made from steel, stainless steel, non-ferrous metal, brass, or aluminum alloy. The standard also references related Indian standards for surface discontinuities and general requirements.

IS 2907 : 1998.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2907 : 1998 NON-FERROUS RIVETS (1.6 TO 10 mm) (First Revision)
IS 2907 : 1998.scope: This standard specifies requirements for non-ferrous rivets made of copper, tinned copper, brass, and aluminium, with nominal diameters ranging from 1.6 mm to 10 mm, intended for general engineering purposes. It covers snap head, flat countersunk head, and flat head rivets with specified dimensions and designations. Rivets must conform to manufacturing and testing requirements as outlined.

IS 2998 : 1982.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2998 : 1982 COLD FORGED STEEL RIVETS FOR COLD CLOSING (1 TO 16 MM DIAMETER)
IS 2998 : 1982.scope: This standard covers cold forged steel rivets with diameters ranging from 1 to 16 mm, intended for general engineering purposes. It specifies requirements for materials, dimensions, tolerances, and tests for these rivets. The rivets are designed for cold closing applications in structural and mechanical assemblies.

IS 3063 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3063 : 1994 FASTENERS-SINGLE COIL RECTANGULAR SECTION SPRING LOCK WASHERS (Second Revision)
IS 3063 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements for single coil rectangular section spring lock washers suitable for use with bolt/nut assemblies involving fasteners of property class 5.8 or less in the size range 2 to 100 mm. These washers are designed to prevent loosening and provide a secure fit in fastener assemblies.

IS 3121 : 1981.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3121 : 1981 RIGGING SCREWS AND STRETCHING SCREWS (First Revision)
IS 3121 : 1981.scope: This standard covers requirements for materials, dimensions, finishing, and tests of rigging screws (M12 to M90) and stretching screws (M6 to M52). It specifies dimensions, proof loads, galvanizing requirements, and workmanship for these fasteners used in structural applications.

IS 3468 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3468 : 1991 PIPE NUTS (Second Revision)
IS 3468 : 1991.scope: This standard specifies requirements for pipe nuts in the size range G 1/8 to G6. It covers thread sizes, material properties, and designation rules for pipe nuts made from steel or brass, with specific mechanical properties and tolerances as per Indian Standards.

IS 3757 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3757 : 1985 HIGH STRENGTH STRUCTURAL BOLTS (Second Revision)
IS 3757 : 1985.scope: This standard covers high strength structural bolts in property classes 8.8 and 10.9, with thread sizes ranging from M16 to M36, designed for use in both friction-type and bearing-type structural steel joints. The bolts are specified to provide assurance against failure by thread stripping due to overtightening when matched with appropriate nuts.

IS 4762 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4762 : 1984 WORM DRIVE CLAMPS FOR GENERAL PURPOSE (First Revision)
IS 4762 : 1984.scope: This standard specifies requirements for worm drive hose clamps intended for general purposes. It covers dimensions, materials, manufacturing, and testing procedures to ensure secure, reliable, and corrosion-resistant performance. The clamps are designed to fit various hose sizes and must pass specific torque and pressure tests.

IS 5369 : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR PLAIN WASHERS AND LOCK WASHERS
IS 5369 : 1975.scope: This standard outlines general requirements and permissible deviations for plain washers, lock washers, and similar parts, specifying grades (precision and ordinary) and finish options such as natural, phosphate-coated, nickel-plated, tinned, galvanized, copper-plated, or cadmium-coated. It also mandates that these parts be free from cracks, burrs, pits, and sharp edges.

IS 5372 : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5372 : 1975 TAPER WASHERS FOR CHANNELS (ISMC) (First Revision)
IS 5372 : 1975.scope: This standard specifies requirements for taper washers designed for use with Indian Standard Medium Weight Channels (ISMC) and bolts with diameters ranging from 10 to 39 mm. It covers dimensions and designation rules for these washers, which are intended for structural applications involving channels.

IS 5373 : 1969.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5373 : 1969 SQUARE WASHERS FOR WOOD FASTENINGS
IS 5373 : 1969.scope: This standard specifies requirements for square washers intended for use in wood fastenings with bolts having a diameter range of 10 to 52 mm. It covers dimensions and designation of square washers for various bolt sizes, including nominal sizes and alternative sizes in brackets. The standard also references additional requirements for plain and lock washers to IS 5369:1975.

IS 5374 : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5374 : 1975 TAPER WASHERS FOR I-BEAMS (ISMB) (First Revision)
IS 5374 : 1975.scope: This standard covers taper washers designed for use with Indian Standard Medium Weight Beams (ISMB) and bolts ranging from 10 mm to 39 mm in diameter. It specifies dimensions and designation rules for these washers, which are used in structural connections. The standard also references related specifications for plain and lock washers.

IS 5624 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF FOUNDATION BOLTS (First Revision)
IS 5624 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements for foundation bolts in the size range M8 to M72, covering material, dimensions, mechanical properties, and designation rules. It includes specifications for hexagon head bolts with or without hexagon nuts, and options for hot-dip galvanization. The standard also references related Indian Standards for thread standards, mechanical properties, and shank forms.

IS 6113 : 1970.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6113 : 1970 ALUMINIUM FASTENERS FOR BUILDING PURPOSES
IS 6113 : 1970.scope: This standard covers requirements for J-type hook bolts, mushroom head seam bolts, nuts, and washers made of aluminium for roofing sheets. It specifies material, grade, dimensions, and designation for these fasteners used in building applications.

IS 6610 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6610 : 1972 HEAVY WASHERS FOR STEEL STRUCTURES
IS 6610 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies requirements for heavy washers used in steel structures with a nominal diameter range of 14 to 42 mm. These washers are designed to be compatible with specific bolt sizes and must be made from steel. Detailed specifications and general requirements for plain and lock washers are referenced to other standards.

IS 6623 : 2004.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6623 : 2004 HIGH STRENGTH STRUCTURAL NUTS (Second Revision)
IS 6623 : 2004.scope: This standard specifies requirements for large series hexagon high strength structural nuts in property classes 8 and 10, suitable for use in both friction-type and bearing-type structural steel connections. The nuts are designed to provide a high level of assurance against failure by thread stripping on overtightening when matched with appropriate bolts.

IS 6639 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6639 : 1972 HEXAGON BOLTS FOR STEEL STRUCTURES
IS 6639 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hexagon bolts used in steel structures with sizes ranging from 12 mm to 39 mm. It covers dimensions, mechanical properties, and designation of bolts, including preferred lengths and grades. The bolts are designed for structural applications and are typically galvanized for corrosion resistance.

IS 6649 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF HARDENED AND TEMPERED WASHERS FOR HIGH STRENGTH STRUCTURAL BOLTS AND NUTS
IS 6649 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements for hardened and tempered steel washers intended for assembly with high strength structural bolts and nuts in the size range M16 to M36. The washers are categorized into plain hole, square taper (6° and 8°), and are made from 45C8 steel. They conform to specific dimensional, hardness, and finish requirements for structural applications.

IS 6733 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6733 : 1972 WALL AND ROOFING NAILS
IS 6733 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for wall and roofing nails, including dimensions, designation, general requirements, finish, and test methods. It covers both wall nails and roofing nails with specific size, head diameter, length, and nail count per kilogram. The nails must be machine-made, free from wastes, and have proper head and shank characteristics.

IS 6736 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6736 : 1972 SLOTTED RAISED COUNTERSUNK HEAD WOOD SCREWS
IS 6736 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies requirements for slotted raised countersunk head wood screws. It covers dimensions, preferred lengths, and head diameter specifications for various screw sizes. The standard also references additional details for tolerances and detailed dimensions.

IS 6739 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6739 : 1972 SLOTTED ROUND HEAD WOOD SCREWS
IS 6739 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies requirements for slotted round head wood screws. It covers dimensions, preferred lengths, and designation rules for these fasteners. The standard is intended for use in wood construction applications.

IS 6760 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6760 : 1972 SLOTTED COUNTERSUNK HEAD WOOD SCREWS
IS 6760 : 1972.scope: This standard specifies requirements for slotted countersunk head wood screws. It covers dimensions, preferred lengths, and designation rules for these fasteners. The screws are designed for use in wood applications, with specific dimensional tolerances and material specifications.

IS 8033 : 1976.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8033 : 1976 WASHERS WITH SQUARE HOLE FOR WOOD FASTENINGS
IS 8033 : 1976.scope: This standard specifies requirements for washers with square holes used in wood fastenings with bolts ranging from 6 to 24 mm in diameter. These washers are designed for ordinary grade as per IS 5369 and are intended for use in wood fastening applications.

IS 8412 : 1977.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8412 : 1977 SLOTTED COUNTERSUNK HEAD BOLTS FOR STEEL STRUCTURES
IS 8412 : 1977.scope: This standard specifies requirements for slotted countersunk head bolts used in steel structures with nominal diameters ranging from 10 to 24 mm. It covers dimensions, grades, designations, mechanical properties, and general requirements for bolts and associated nuts. The bolts are typically black grade (B) and conform to prescribed mechanical properties.

IS 8869 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8869 : 1978 WASHERS FOR CORRUGATED SHEET ROOFING
IS 8869 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for washers used in corrugated sheet roofing. It covers bituminous felt, steel, and lead washers, detailing their types, dimensions, and designations. The standard ensures proper material properties and manufacturing processes for roofing applications.

IS 10238 : 2001.title: FASTENERS – STEPS BOLT FOR STEEL STRUCTURES
IS 10238 : 2001.scope: This standard covers the requirements for step bolts used in steel structures including transmission towers to gain access to the top. It specifies dimensions, grades, mechanical properties, mating nuts and washers, and finish requirements for these fasteners.

IS 12427 : 2001.title: FASTENERS – THREADED STEEL HEXAGON HEAD TRANSMISSION TOWER BOLTS (First Revision)
IS 12427 : 2001.scope: Covers the requirements for hot-dip galvanized hexagon head transmission tower bolts in the size range M12 to M24 for use in the construction of transmission towers, sub-stations and similar steel structures. The bolts covered in this standard are not suitable for applications requiring improved low temperature characteristics.

IS 278 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 278 : 1978 GALVANIZED STEEL BARBED WIRE FOR FENCING (Third Revision)
IS 278 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for two types of galvanized steel barbed wire used in fencing. It covers the material, construction, dimensions, and mechanical properties of the wire, including barb design and coating specifications. The standard ensures the wire is free from defects and meets tensile and ductility requirements for durability in fencing applications.

IS 2140 : 1978.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2140 : 1978 STRANDED GALVANIZED STEEL WIRE FOR FENCING (First Revision)
IS 2140 : 1978.scope: This standard specifies requirements for galvanized stranded steel wire used in fencing, covering 3-ply and 7-ply constructions. It defines nominal diameters, minimum breaking loads, lay lengths, and material specifications for the wire. The wire must conform to specified tensile strength and galvanizing requirements.

IS 2365 : 1977.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2365 : 1977 STEEL WIRE SUSPENSION ROPES FOR LIFTS, ELEVATORS AND HOISTS (First Revision)
IS 2365 : 1977.scope: This standard covers requirements for steel wire ropes used in lifts, elevators, and hoists carrying passengers or goods, and working in guides. It does not apply to ropes used for winding purposes in mines. The standard specifies construction, size ranges, breaking loads, and core materials for various rope designs.

IS 2721 : 1979.title: Galvanized Steel Chain Link Fence Fabric
IS 2721 : 1979.scope: This standard specifies requirements for galvanized steel chain link fence fabric intended for various purposes. It covers the fabric itself but does not include requirements for straining posts, struts, or base plates.

IS 2553 (PART 1) : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF SAFETY GLASS PART 1 GENERAL PURPOSE (Third Revision)
IS 2553 (PART 1) : 1990.scope: This standard specifies requirements for safety glass intended for general purposes such as glazing windows, doors of buildings, and railway coaches. It covers different types including toughened safety glass, toughened float safety glass, laminated safety glass, and laminated float safety glass. The standard outlines material, thickness, dimensional tolerances, and test requirements for these glass products.

IS 2835 : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2835 : 1987 FLAT TRANSPARENT SHEET GLASS (Third Revision)
IS 2835 : 1987.scope: This standard covers requirements and methods of sampling and testing for flat transparent sheet glass used in the manufacture of photographic plates, projection slides, silvered glass mirrors, toughened or laminated safety glasses, and for glazing and framing purposes. It also includes specifications for greenhouse quality glass for glazing and flooring applications.

IS 3438 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF SILVERED GLASS MIRRORS FOR GENERAL PURPOSES (Second Revision)
IS 3438 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for silvered glass mirrors used for general purposes, including glass sheet, silvering, copper coating, and protective paint. It ensures the mirrors have minimum silver and copper deposits, are free from defects, and pass specified tests for durability and reflectance.

IS 5437 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 5437 : 1994 FIGURED, ROLLED AND WIRED GLASS (First Revision)
IS 5437 : 1994.scope: This standard provides summary requirements and sampling methods for figured, rolled, and wired glass. It specifies general requirements such as absence of cracks and stones, and details thickness, dimensional tolerances, wire mesh specifications, and flameproofness for wired glass.

IS 110 : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 110 : 1983 READY MIXED PAINT, BRUSHING, GREY FILLER, FOR ENAMELS FOR USE OVER PRIMERS (Second Revision)
IS 110 : 1983.scope: This standard covers requirements and testing methods for ready mixed paint, brushing, and grey filler used over primers in painting systems for enamels. The material is typically applied as a filler over the primer before enamels are applied. It specifies composition, flattening properties, hold-out, drying time, consistency, finish, colour, residue, flexibility, and flash point requirements.

IS 419 : 1967.title: PUTTY, FOR USE ON WINDOW FRAMES
IS 419 : 1967.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for putty used in fixing glass panes on wood and metal frames, and for filling splits, cracks, and holes in wood or metal. The material must be homogeneous, free from impurities, and have good plasticity for easy application without crumbling or cracking.

IS 423 : 1961.title: SUMMARY OF IS 423 : 1961 PLASTIC WOOD FOR JOINERS FILLER (Revised)
IS 423 : 1961.scope: This standard covers plastic wood for joiners filler, a material used to fill holes, cracks, and irregularities in wood to produce a smooth surface suitable for staining. It specifies requirements, composition, and performance characteristics for the product.

IS 3709 : 1966.title: Mastic Cement for Bedding of Metal Window
IS 3709 : 1966.scope: This standard specifies the requirements and methods of sampling and testing for mastic cement intended for bedding metal windows into wooden frames, metal frames into masonry or concrete, and for bonding metal frames to other materials. The material is designed to be applied by hand or with a putty knife and is expected to be suitable for taking paint without lifting, bleeding, or cracking.

IS 3677 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3677 : 1985 UNBONDED ROCK AND SLAG WOOL FOR THERMAL INSULATION (Second Revision)
IS 3677 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies the requirements and testing methods for unbonded rock and slag wool used as thermal insulation materials. It covers both loose and stitched types, detailing properties like apparent density, thermal conductivity, and resistance to various environmental factors. The standard aims to ensure consistent quality for thermal insulation applications.

IS 4671 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4671 : 1984 EXPANDED POLYSTYRENE FOR THERMAL INSULATION PURPOSES (First Revision)
IS 4671 : 1984.scope: This standard covers expanded polystyrene in the form of rough shapes, finished boards and blocks, and pipe sections/segments for thermal insulation in refrigeration and building applications within a temperature range of 150°C to 80°C.

IS 6598 : 1972.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6598 : 1972 CELLULAR CONCRETE FOR THERMAL INSULATION
IS 6598 : 1972.scope: Cellular concrete, formed by producing gas or air bubbles in cement or cement-sand slurry, is a versatile construction material for thermal insulation due to its light weight, resistance to sulphate action, alternate cooling and thawing, and resistance to water penetration. It can be applied over flat roofs for thermal insulation and is available in two types: high pressure steam cured (precast) or cured under ambient conditions (in situ or precast).

IS 7509 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 7509 : 1993 THERMAL INSULATING CEMENT (First Revision)
IS 7509 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies the requirements and testing methods for thermal insulating cements used at temperatures up to 950°C. It defines three types (350°C, 750°C, and 950°C) based on their service temperature range. The material must be in the form of dry powder or granulated aggregate and must meet specific performance criteria as outlined in Table 1.

IS 8154 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8154 : 1993 PREFORMED CALCIUM SILICATE INSULATION (FOR TEMPERATURES UPTO 650 °C)
IS 8154 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for preformed calcium silicate insulation intended for use on surfaces that reach temperatures up to 650°C. It covers material composition, physical properties, thermal performance, and dimensional tolerances for various shapes including flat blocks, bevelled lags, and pipe sections.

IS 8183 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8183 : 1993 BONDED MINERAL WOOL (First Revision)
IS 8183 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies the requirements and testing methods for bonded mineral wool, a thermal insulation material made from rock, slag, or glass processed into a fibrous form and bonded with a suitable binder. It covers properties such as bulk density, thermal conductivity, moisture content, and dimensional tolerances for use in thermal insulation applications.

IS 9428 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9428 : 1993 PREFORMED CALCIUM SILICATE INSULATION (FOR TEMPERATURES UP TO 950°C) (First Revision)
IS 9428 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for preformed calcium silicate insulation intended for use on surfaces with temperatures up to 950°C. It covers bulk density, compressive strength, flexural strength, heat resistance, thermal conductivity, moisture content, and dimensional tolerances for various shapes including flat blocks, bevelled lags, pipe sections, and curved segments.

IS 9742 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9742 : 1993 SPRAYED MINERAL WOOL THERMAL INSULATION (First Revision)
IS 9742 : 1993.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for sprayed mineral wool thermal insulation, including material specifications, physical properties, fire resistance, and performance criteria for applications in building insulation.

IS 9743 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9743 : 1990 THERMAL INSULATION FINISHING CEMENTS (First Revision)
IS 9743 : 1990.scope: This standard covers thermal insulation finishing cements used for insulating piping systems. These cements are prepared by mixing with water and applied at the plant. The standard specifies requirements for different types of cements, including hard-setting, self-setting, gypsum plaster, and fire-proof types, for various applications such as turbine insulation and pipe covering.

IS 9842 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9842 : 1994 PREFORMED FIBROUS PIPE INSULATION (First Revision)
IS 9842 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies the requirements for preformed fibrous pipe insulation made from mineral wool, including thermal conductivity, bulk density, shot content, moisture absorption, and dimensional tolerances. It covers insulation for pipes and tubing, with specifications for different groups and nominal thicknesses. The material must be incombustible and meet performance criteria for heat resistance and mechanical properties.

IS 11128 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 11128 : 1984 SPRAY - APPLIED HYDRATED CALCIUM SILICATE THERMAL INSULATION
IS 11128 : 1984.scope: This standard covers spray-applied hydrated calcium silicate thermal insulation materials, specifying requirements for composition, density, compressive strength, thermal conductivity, adhesion, heat resistance, moisture content, and fire protection. It defines the material's suitability for thermal insulation applications under specified conditions.

IS 11307 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 11307 : 1985 CELLULAR GLASS BLOCK AND PIPE THERMAL INSULATION
IS 11307 : 1985.scope: This standard covers cellular glass block and pipe thermal insulation materials intended for use on surfaces operating between -200°C and 425°C. The materials must be formed from a glass composition, annealed, and set to form rigid, incombustible, hermetically sealed cells. They are supplied in standard or commercial sizes with specified dimensions and tolerances.

IS 11308 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF IS 11308 : 1985 HYDRAULIC SETTING THERMAL INSULATING CASTABLES FOR TEMPERATURE UPTO 1250°C
IS 11308 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements and methods for hydraulic setting thermal insulating castables used as hot or cold face backing for refractory linings at temperatures up to 1250°C. It covers two types: Type 1050 for up to 1050°C and Type 1250 for up to 1250°C, with detailed physical and thermal properties.

IS 12436 : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12436 : 1988 PREFORMED RIGID POLYURETHANE (PUR) AND POLYISOCYANURATE (PIR) FOAMS FOR THERMAL INSULATION
IS 12436 : 1988.scope: This standard covers preformed rigid polyurethane (PUR) and polyisocyanurate (PIR) foams for thermal insulation, specifying their types, grades, compositions, and requirements for use in construction. The materials are available in various forms including boards, slabs, pipe sections, and lags, with different grades for general use or high compressive resistance.

IS 13204 : 1991.title: SUMMARY OF IS 13204 : 1991 RIGID PHENOLIC FOAM FOR THERMAL INSULATION
IS 13204 : 1991.scope: This standard covers requirements and testing methods for rigid phenolic foam used as thermal insulation. It applies to slab blocks, boards, and profiled sections (pipe sections and radiused or bevelled lags) cut from pipes. The material is typically supplied with craft paper facing on both sides.

IS 2036 : 1995.title: SUMMARY OF PHENOLIC LAMINATED SHEETS (Second Revision)
IS 2036 : 1995.scope: This standard covers phenolic resin bonded laminated sheets of one class, including those with asbestos, woven cotton fabric, and cellulose paper reinforcements. It specifies requirements for appearance, flatness, thickness, machinability, and physical/electrical properties for sheets with nominal thicknesses from 0.4 mm to 100 mm.

IS 2508 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2508 : 1984 LOW DENSITY POLYTHYLENE FILMS (Second Revision)
IS 2508 : 1984.scope: This standard covers low density polyethylene films intended for packaging, canal lining, agricultural operations, and post-harvest uses. The films are flexible, unsupported, and come in various thicknesses and widths. Coloured films other than black require agreement between supplier and purchaser.

IS 6307 : 1985.title: SUMMARY OF RIGID PVC SHEETS (First Revision)
IS 6307 : 1985.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for rigid PVC sheets with thicknesses from 0.10 to 12.5 mm, manufactured by calendaring, extrusion, or calendaring followed by lamination. It covers types suitable for general purpose, specified impact strength, and deep draw vacuum forming applications.

IS 10889 : 1984.title: SUMMARY OF HIGH DENSITY POLYTHYLENE FILM
IS 10889 : 1984.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for high density polyethylene (HDPE) films, including grades 1 and 2, for various applications. It covers dimensional, physical, and mechanical properties such as thickness, width, tensile strength, elongation, and impact resistance. The film can be natural or black (carbon black pigmented) and is used in applications requiring specific performance characteristics.

IS 12830 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12830 : 1989 RUBBER BASED ADHESIVE FOR FIXING PVC TILES TO CEMENT
IS 12830 : 1989.scope: This standard specifies requirements and testing methods for rubber-based adhesives used to bond PVC tiles to cement floors and walls in buildings. It covers material composition, consistency, assembly time, adhesion strength, and storage conditions.

IS 12994 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF IS 12994 : 1990 EPOXY ADHESIVES, ROOM TEMPERATURE CURING, GENERAL PURPOSE
IS 12994 : 1990.scope: This standard covers requirements and testing methods for liquid and paste type epoxy adhesives used for general-purpose applications. It specifies performance criteria for four types of adhesives (L50, P50, L100, P100) based on their operating temperature ranges. The standard defines properties of uncured and cured adhesive joints, including gelation time, viscosity, spreadability, sagging, and tensile shear strength.

IS 14182 : 1994.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14182 : 1994 SOLVENT CEMENT FOR USE WITH UNPLASTICIZED POLYVINYL CHLORIDE PLASTIC PIPE AND FITTINGS
IS 14182 : 1994.scope: This standard specifies requirements and methods for solvent cement used in joining unplasticized polyvinyl chloride (U-PVC) pipes and fittings intended for carrying potable water, including pressure or non-pressure type pipes. It covers the formulation, physical properties, and performance criteria of the cement to ensure proper bonding and durability.

IS 1443 : 1997.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1443 : 1997 POLYCARBONATE SHEETS
IS 1443 : 1997.scope: This standard covers polycarbonate sheets of solid section as well as multi-wall variety and thinner gauge sheets (films), multi-layer composite laminates of polycarbonate, compact sheets and composites of polycarbonate glass fibre or any other reinforcement. It establishes a system for designating various possible polycarbonate sheets and films based on their structure and properties, not on application or end use.

IS 14643 : 1999.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14643 : 1999 UNSINTERED POLYTFE TAPE FOR THREADS SEALING APPLICATIONS
IS 14643 : 1999.scope: This standard covers unsintered polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) tape for thread sealing applications in pipes, suitable for use with common fluids and gases under ambient conditions up to 80 bar gauge for pipe sizes up to 50 mm, with operating temperature range of -20°C to 200°C.

IS 14753 : 1999.title: SUMMARY OF IS 14753 : 1999 POLYMETHYL METHACRYLATE (PMMA) (ACRYLIC) SHEETS
IS 14753 : 1999.scope: This standard specifies the requirements, sampling methods, and tests for polymethyl methacrylate (acrylic) sheets. It covers surface protection, appearance, dimensions, thickness, refractive index, and mechanical properties to ensure quality for intended applications.

IS 694 : 1990.title: SUMMARY OF IS 694 : 1990 PVC INSULATED CABLE FOR WORKING VOLTAGES UP TO AND INCLUDING 1100 VOLTS (Third Revision)
IS 694 : 1990.scope: This standard covers PVC insulated cables for working voltages up to and including 1100 volts, including fixed wiring and flexible cords with copper or aluminum conductors. It specifies requirements for materials, construction, and tests for various cable types such as single-core, twin, and flat cables, both sheathed and un sheathed.

IS 1554 (PART 1) : 1988.title: SUMMARY OF IS 1554 (PART 1) : 1988 PVC INSULATED (HEAVY DUTY) ELECTRIC CABLES
IS 1554 (PART 1) : 1988.scope: This standard covers PVC insulated, armoured and unarmoured single-core, twin-core, three-core and multi-core cables for electric supply and control purposes, suitable for working voltages up to and including 1100 volts. These cables are designed for continuous operation and can be used in both earthed and unearthing systems, including d.c. systems up to 1500 V.

IS 7098 (PART 1) : 1988.title: CROSSLINKED POLYETHYLENE INSULATED THERMOPLASTIC SHEATHED CABLES PART-1 FOR WORKING VOLTAGES UP TO AND INCLUDING 1100 VOLTS
IS 7098 (PART 1) : 1988.scope: This standard covers cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) insulated and PVC sheathed cables for electric supply and control purposes, suitable for AC or DC systems up to 1100 volts. It includes both armoured and unarmoured cables, with additional requirements for mining applications and fire performance categories C1 and C2.

IS 9968 (PART 1) : 1998.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9968 (PART 1) : 1998 ELASTOMER INSULATED CABLES, PART1 FOR WORKING VOLTAGES UPTO AND INCLUDING 1100 VOLTS
IS 9968 (PART 1) : 1998.scope: This standard covers elastomeric insulated cables and cords for fixed wiring, flexible cables, and flexible cords for electric power and lighting at voltages up to and including 1100 volts. It specifies requirements for various types of cables including braided and compounded/varnished, elastomer sheathed, and flexible cords, suitable for single-phase or three-phase systems.

IS 1293 : 1988.title: PLUGS AND SOCKET-OUTLETS – RATED VOLTAGE UPTO AND INCLUDING 250 VOLTS AND RATED CURRENT UPTO AND INCLUDING 16 AMPERES
IS 1293 : 1988.scope: This standard covers three-pin two-pole and earthing plugs and socket-outlets, including multi-socket-outlets, suitable for AC circuits with rated voltage above 50 V but not exceeding 250 V and rated current of 6 A or 16 A. It specifies requirements for materials, dimensions, and tests for these electrical components.

IS 2086 : 1993.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2086 : 1993 CARRIERS AND BASES USED IN REWIRABLE TYPE ELECTRIC FUSES FOR VOLTAGES UP TO 650 VOLTS (Third Revision)
IS 2086 : 1993.scope: This standard covers performance requirements and dimensions of carriers and bases used in rewirable type electric fuses for voltages up to 650V. It specifies electrical, physical, and mechanical requirements for fuse carriers and bases, including rated currents, breaking capacity, and material properties. The standard applies to fuses rated for 240V single-phase and 415V three-phase systems.

IS 2412 : 1975.title: SUMMARY OF IS 2412 : 1975 LINK CLIPS FOR ELECTRICAL WIRING (First Revision)
IS 2412 : 1975.scope: This standard covers requirements and tests for link clips, including both joint link clips and link clips with separate linking eyes, for general electrical wiring purposes. It specifies material, construction, dimensions, and testing procedures for these clips.

IS 3419 : 1989.title: SUMMARY OF FITTINGS FOR RIGID NON-METALLIC CONDUITS (Second Revision)
IS 3419 : 1989.scope: This standard covers requirements and methods of test for rigid conduit fittings manufactured from insulating materials suitable for use with circular, rigid, non-flame propagating, and non-threadable plain conduits of insulating materials. The fittings covered include couplers, bends, elbows, tees, inspection sleeves, and boxes, designed for temperature ranges between -5°C and +60°C.

IS 3480 : 1966.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3480 : 1966 FLEXIBLE STEEL CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL WIRING
IS 3480 : 1966.scope: This standard specifies requirements for flexible steel conduits used for protection of cables in electrical installations. The conduits must be made from mild steel with protective coatings and must meet dimensional and mechanical test criteria to ensure durability and safety in electrical wiring applications.

IS 3837 : 1976.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3837 : 1976 ACCESSORIES FOR RIGID STEEL CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL WIRING (First Revision)
IS 3837 : 1976.scope: This standard covers accessories for rigid steel conduits conforming to IS 9537 (Part 2) 1981, including clips, saddles, pipe hooks, crampets, plugs, lock nuts, and bushes. It specifies material, dimensions, and protective coatings for these components used in electrical wiring systems.

IS 3854 : 1997.title: SUMMARY OF IS 3854 : 1997 SWITCHES FOR DOMESTIC AND SIMILAR PURPOSES (Second Revision)
IS 3854 : 1997.scope: This standard covers manually operated general purpose switches for household and similar fixed electrical installations, including those with screwless terminals and pilot lights. It applies to switches rated for voltages up to 440V and currents up to 63A, but excludes circuit breakers, appliances, and switches in hazardous locations.

IS 4160 : 1967.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4160 : 1967 INTERLOCKING SWITCH SOCKET OUTLET
IS 4160 : 1967.scope: This standard specifies requirements for interlocking type switch socket outlets rated up to 30A for use in AC and DC circuits at voltages up to 250 V. It covers materials, construction, and performance tests for these electrical devices.

IS 4615 : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF IS 4615 : 1968 SWITCH SOCKET-OUTLETS (NON-INTERLOCKING TYPE)
IS 4615 : 1968.scope: This standard specifies requirements for non-interlocking type switch socket outlets, including surface and flush types, rated up to 30 A and suitable for AC and DC circuits at voltages not exceeding 250 V. It covers materials, dimensions, and performance tests for these electrical components.

IS 4649 : 1968.title: SUMMARY OF ADAPTORS FOR FLEXIBLE STEEL CONDUITS
IS 4649 : 1968.scope: This standard covers requirements for adaptors of clamp and solid types for flexible steel conduits intended for the protection of cables in electrical installations. Adaptors are used for connecting flexible metallic conduits to a rigid conduit or its fitting. Flexible steel conduits shall not be used as earth continuity conductor.

IS 6538 : 1971.title: SUMMARY OF IS 6538 : 1971 THREE-PIN PLUGS MADE OF RESILIENT MATERIAL
IS 6538 : 1971.scope: This standard specifies requirements and tests for three-pin plugs made of resilient material, suitable for use in AC and DC circuits up to 250 V, conforming to IS 1293:1988. These plugs are intended for use with socket outlets and must have specific dimensions and material properties.

IS 8828 : 1996.title: SUMMARY OF IS 8828 : 1996/IEC 898 (1995) CIRCUIT BREAKERS FOR OVER CURRENT PROTECTION FOR HOUSEHOLD AND SIMILAR INSTALLATIONS (Second Revision)
IS 8828 : 1996.scope: This standard covers A.C. air-break circuit-breakers for household and similar installations, designed for protection against overcurrents and intended for use by un instructed people. It applies to circuit-breakers with rated voltages not exceeding 440 V, rated currents not exceeding 125 A, and short-circuit capacities not exceeding 25 000 A.

IS 9537 (PART 1) : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL INSTALLATIONS PART 1 - GENERAL REQUIREMENTS
IS 9537 (PART 1) : 1980.scope: This standard (Part I) applies to conduits for the protection of conductors and/or cables in electrical installations. Conduits shall be designed and constructed to ensure reliable mechanical protection to the conductors and/or cables during transport, storage, recommended practice and usage.

IS 9537 (Part 1) : 1980.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9537(PART II) : 1981 CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL WIRING PART 2 RIGID STEEL CONDUITS
IS 9537 (Part 1) : 1980.scope: This standard covers threaded/threadable plain rigid steel conduits designed to provide reliable mechanical protection for conductors and/or cables during transport, storage, and usage. Conduits must withstand stresses likely to occur in practice.

IS 9537 (Part 3) : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL INSTALLATIONS PART 3 – RIGID PLAIN CONDUITS OF INSULATING MATERIALS
IS 9537 (Part 3) : 1983.scope: This standard covers circular rigid non-flame propagating and non-threadable plain conduits of insulating materials, specifically for electrical installations. It outlines requirements and test methods for these conduits, including classification based on mechanical properties and dimensional specifications. The standard applies to conduits with light, medium, and heavy mechanical stress ratings.

IS 9537 (Part 4) : 1983.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9537 (PART 4) : 1983 CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL INSTALLATIONS PART 4 – PLIABLE SELF RECOVERING CONDUITS OF INSULATING MATERIALS
IS 9537 (Part 4) : 1983.scope: This standard covers the requirements and testing methods for pliable self-recovering plain and corrugated conduits made of insulating materials, intended for electrical installations. It specifies mechanical properties and dimensions for conduits classified by stress levels. Provision of IS 9537 (Part 1):1980 applies for general requirements.

IS 9537 (PART 5) : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF IS 9537 (PART 5) : 2000 CONDUITS FOR ELECTRICAL INSTALLATIONS PART 5 – PLIABLE CONDUITS OF INSULATING MATERIAL
IS 9537 (PART 5) : 2000.scope: This standard specifies requirements for pliable non-flame propagating conduits of insulating material, including corrugated conduits with a smooth exterior surface. It does not include self-recovering or flexible conduits and applies to both plain and corrugated types.

IS 14772 : 2000.title: SUMMARY OF GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR ENCLOSURES FOR ACCESSORIES AND SIMILAR FIXED ELECTRICAL INSTALLATIONS – SPECIFICATION
IS 14772 : 2000.scope: This standard applies to enclosures or parts for accessories with a rated voltage not exceeding 440 V intended for household or similar fixed electrical installations, either indoors or outdoors. It provides general requirements for enclosures designed to protect against external influences and ensure adequate electrical and mechanical protection to the user or surroundings.

IS 875 (PART 1) : 1987.title: SUMMARY OF IS 875 (PART 1) : 1987 CODE OF PRACTICE FOR DESIGN LOADS (OTHER THAN EARTHQUAKE) FOR BUILDINGS AND STRUCTURES, PART 1 DEAD LOADS - UNIT WEIGHTS OF BUILDING MATERIALS
IS 875 (PART 1) : 1987.scope: This standard covers the unit weight mass of materials and components in buildings for the determination of dead loads in design. It specifies unit weights of materials likely to be stored in buildings for load calculations, including internal friction where appropriate.
